confirmed.title: NoteService.init recovery re-dispatches live in-flight auto-action dispatches, causing duplicate workflow runs
confirmed.detail: Finding is accurate as written. One minor path correction: the node addon adapter is Sources/RielaCLI/ProductionNodeAdapter+NoteAddons.swift (RielaCLI, not RielaCore); the cited line 108 is correct.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Only recover dispatches whose updated_at is older than a staleness window (e.g. > max expected workflow duration), or record a process/session owner token in the row and only recover rows owned by dead sessions; alternatively move recovery out of init into an explicit maintenance command.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. recoverInterruptedAutoActionDispatches (Sources/RielaNote/AutoActionDispatching.swift:208-227) resets ALL in_flight rows (attempt_count < 3) to pending with no staleness threshold or ownership check, and NoteService.init (Sources/RielaNote/NoteService.swift:26-29) then calls retryPendingAutoActionDispatches, which synchronously re-dispatches them. A row genuinely stays in_flight for the entire workflow run: NoteAutoActionTaskLauncher.launch (Sources/RielaCLI/NoteAutoActionWorkflowDispatcher.swift:18-25, 175-188) blocks on a semaphore until runner.run finishes and only then marks the row dispatched. Both trigger paths exist: every riela note CLI command builds a fresh NoteService with a live dispatcher (Sources/RielaCLI/NoteCommands.swift:583-590), and note addon nodes do the same mid-workflow (Sources/RielaCLI/ProductionNodeAdapter+NoteAddons.swift:108-112), so a second CLI invocation or the auto-tagging workflow's own note addon re-launches a duplicate concurrent run of the same workflow for the same note (bounded only by attempt cap 3). Default AI-tagging actions are seeded enabled with NULL filter (NoteStoreSchema.swift:77-94), so no configuration is needed. The completion UPDATE keyed only on status='in_flight' (no attempt token) also mis-attributes completion across attempts as described. The originatingActionId guard only blocks new event cascades, not recovery of existing rows; no other guard prevents this.

confirmed.title: Server crash: unchecked Double-to-Int conversion for limit/offset arguments
confirmed.detail: Accurate as written. Minor precision: the .integer(Int64) branch (line 774) is safe on 64-bit platforms (Int == Int64); the defect is solely the .number(Double) branch at line 782. The trap message on out-of-range positive values is "Double value cannot be converted to Int because the result would be greater than Int.max".
confirmed.fix_sketch: Guard the range before converting: `guard value >= Double(Int.min), value <= Double(Int.max) else { throw .invalidVariable(...) }`, or use `Int(exactly: value)` and throw invalidVariable on nil. Also treat non-numeric values as invalidVariable instead of silently returning nil.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed. optionalInt() at Sources/RielaGraphQL/NoteGraphQLDocumentExecutor.swift:772-783 handles the .number(Double) case by checking only integrality (value.rounded(.towardZero) == value) and then calling Int(value) at line 782 with no range check. Swift's Int(Double) traps for out-of-range values; 1e300 is finite and integral so it passes the guard and reaches Int(1e300), which traps (verified by compiling/running the exact sequence in Swift 6.3.3 -> Fatal error, exit 133). The value reaches optionalInt via both attack surfaces: (a) inline GraphQL literals, since parseGraphQLNumber (NoteGraphQLDocumentParsing.swift:545-557) treats e/E exponent notation as floating point and returns .number(Double); (b) the variables object, since JSONValue.init(from:) (RielaCore/JSONValue.swift:67-70) falls back to .number(Double) when Int64 decode fails. The executor copies parsed arguments into routedRequest.variables without value validation (line 101) and the call sites boundedLimit(try optionalInt(...))/boundedOffset(try optionalInt(...)) at lines 126-127,135-136,149-150,155 evaluate optionalInt (and trap) before any clamping runs, on notes/notebooks/searchNotes/proposeNoteLinks. The trap is a fatalError, not a throw, so the do/catch at lines 97-114 cannot contain it; the whole server process dies. JSONValue even has a safe asInt64 helper using Int64(exactly:) that optionalInt fails to use.

confirmed.title: Server crash: Int overflow computing fetchLimit from client-controlled offset in searchNotes
confirmed.detail: searchNotesInDatabase computes `let fetchLimit = requestedLimit + requestedOffset` with unchecked +. The GraphQL executor's boundedOffset() (NoteGraphQLDocumentExecutor.swift:789) only floors offset at 0 and imposes no upper bound, and Int64 literals parse fine, so `query { searchNotes(query: "x", offset: 9223372036854775807) { result { accepted } } }` yields requestedOffset = Int.max; adding the clamped limit (>=1) overflows and traps, crashing the server. notes/notebooks are unaffected because they pass offset straight to SQL OFFSET, but searchNotes always takes this additive path.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Cap offset in the executor (e.g. boundedOffset max of some sane bound) and/or use overflow-safe arithmetic in searchNotesInDatabase (`let fetchLimit = requestedLimit.addingReportingOverflow(requestedOffset)` with clamping).
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the full chain. parseGraphQLNumber (NoteGraphQLDocumentParsing.swift:529) accepts any Int64-fitting literal, so offset: 9223372036854775807 parses to Int64.max; inline arguments become request.variables (executor line 100-101); optionalInt's Int(Int64.max) is lossless on 64-bit; boundedOffset (NoteGraphQLDocumentExecutor.swift:789) only applies max(value, 0) with no upper bound, while boundedLimit floors limit at 1. The value flows unchanged through GraphQLNoteGraphQLService.searchNotes (NoteGraphQLService.swift:72) and NoteService.searchNotes (NoteService.swift:725) into searchNotesInDatabase, where NoteSearch.swift:25 computes requestedLimit + requestedOffset with unchecked +. With requestedOffset = Int.max and requestedLimit >= 1 this overflows and traps fatally; the executor's catch block cannot catch a Swift arithmetic trap, so the process crashes. The executor is server-reachable via Sources/RielaServer/WorkflowServingController.swift:476. No guard, clamp, or test prevents this.

confirmed.title: Stale source-image resolution overwrites the newly selected note's image and state
confirmed.detail: resolveSourceImageAttachment never re-checks selectionGeneration after its awaits, and its callers (prepareSelectedNoteFiles, setContentMode, selectFileAttachment, retrySourceImage) pass no generation. Scenario: select note A whose sourcePageImage resolves slowly (e.g. S3 fetch in NoteServiceRielaNoteUIClient.resolveFile), then immediately select note B. B's selectNote advances selectionGeneration and finishes, but A's in-flight resolveSourceImageAttachment then completes and assigns resolvedSourceImage / selectedResolvedFile / decodedSourceImage (lines 826-828) — so note B's detail view displays note A's page image. On failure it sets state = .failed (line 830), flipping the whole library UI into the error overlay for a note the user already left. selectNote A's tail also sets state = .loaded with no guard after prepareSelectedNoteFiles returns (line 383).
confirmed.fix_sketch: Capture selectionGeneration before the awaits in resolveSourceImageAttachment (or pass it in) and drop results / skip state writes when isCurrentSelection fails; only surface .failed for the current selection.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. resolveSourceImageAttachment (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:819-832) suspends on client.resolveFile (network, line 941) and a detached decode (line 956), then writes resolvedSourceImage/selectedResolvedFile/decodedSourceImage (826-828) and sets global state = .failed on error (830), with no selectionGeneration re-check. The class is @MainActor, so a second selectNote can fully complete during the suspension; none of the callers (prepareSelectedNoteFiles:803, setContentMode:467, retrySourceImage:484, selectFileAttachment:502) pass or check a generation. RielaNoteSourceImageView (RielaNoteComponents.swift:87-92) renders decodedSourceImage unconditionally with no fileId match against the current note, so note A's stale image is displayed for note B; a stale failure flips the whole library state to .failed. selectNote's tail also sets state = .loaded at line 383 with no guard after the await (last guard is line 376), and selectNotebook has the same at line 350. Every specific claim in the finding matches the code.

confirmed.title: loadMoreNotebookNotes appends the previous notebook's page into the newly selected notebook's list
confirmed.detail: loadMoreNotebookNotes captures notebookId at entry, then appendNotebookNotesPage (line 765) writes notebookNotes / notebookNotesOffset / hasMoreNotebookNotes after the await with no selectionGeneration check. Scenario: tap 'Load more' in notebook A, then click notebook B while the page request is in flight. selectNotebook(B) replaces notebookNotes with B's first page; A's request then returns and appends A's notes to B's list, sets notebookNotesOffset to B.count + A-page.count and hasMoreNotebookNotes from A's page — wrong notes shown under B and pagination permanently skewed. The same unguarded write exists inside selectNote: after the await at line 373, loadNotebookNotesFirstPage overwrites notebookNotes even though the isCurrentSelection guard only runs afterwards (line 376), so a superseded selectNote clobbers the list the newer selection just loaded. Additionally, two concurrent invocations (double-click on 'Load more') both read the same offset, fetch the same page, and both add page.count to notebookNotesOffset — the dedup filter hides duplicates but the doubled offset silently skips a full page of notes on the next load.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Capture selectionGeneration in loadMoreNotebookNotes/appendNotebookNotesPage and guard all list/offset writes after each await; add an isLoadingMore reentrancy flag.
confirmed.verify_note: All three sub-claims verified by reading the code. (1) loadMoreNotebookNotes (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:636) -> appendNotebookNotesPage (:765) writes notebookNotes/notebookNotesOffset/hasMoreNotebookNotes after the await with no selectionGeneration guard, while every sibling flow (selectNotebook, selectNote, refresh, performSearch) does guard — so a selectNotebook(B) completing during A's in-flight load-more results in A's page appended to B's list with corrupted offset/hasMore. The class is @MainActor and the view (RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:107-144) fires these in independent unstructured Tasks with no disabling, so the interleaving is reachable. (2) In selectNote, loadNotebookNotesFirstPage at :373 overwrites notebookNotes (:760) after its await, before the isCurrentSelection guard at :376; a superseded selectNote clobbers the newer selection's list, and :374 loadNotebookNotesUntilContains even reads the now-changed selectedNotebookId, mixing the new notebook's pages into the stale list. (3) canLoadMoreNotebookNotes (:160) does not check state==.loading, so a double-tap runs two appends reading the same offset; dedup hides duplicate rows but notebookNotesOffset is incremented twice (:774), skipping a full page on the next load.

confirmed.title: translateSelectedNote overwrites a concurrent user edit with a translation of the stale body (data loss)
confirmed.detail: translateSelectedNote can overwrite a concurrent user edit, but the window is narrower than stated. The Save button handler (RielaNoteDetailView.swift:383-384) calls resetEditingState() -> clearTranslateNoteState() (line 623), which bumps translateNoteGeneration immediately after saveSelectedNoteBody returns, so a translation that completes strictly after the save flow finishes IS caught by the guard at Translation.swift:29. The actual defects: (1) Race during save — if proposeNoteBodyRewrite resumes on the main actor while the save's client.updateNoteBody await is still in flight, translateNoteGeneration is unchanged, the guard passes, and the translation of the stale pre-edit body is written via a second updateNoteBody racing the save's write; if the translation write lands last server-side, the just-saved user edit is silently destroyed. (2) Pre-save overwrite — the Edit button stays enabled during isTranslateNoteLoading and startEditing does not bump the generation, so a translation completing while the user is editing silently persists the stale-body translation under the open editor (the user's later Save then clobbers the translation instead — confusing but not edit-losing). Fix direction stands: bump/invalidate translateNoteGeneration when editing starts and when a save begins (not just after it completes), analogous to markEditRewriteResultStale.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Bump translateNoteGeneration in saveSelectedNoteBody/advanceSelectionGeneration, and/or verify selectedDetail.note.bodyMarkdown still equals the captured originalMarkdown before updateNoteBody; also disable Edit while translating.
confirmed.verify_note: The core hazard is real but the finding's exact sequence is partially wrong. Verified in code: translateSelectedNote (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel+Translation.swift:13-32) captures the pre-edit body and its guard only checks translateNoteGeneration+noteId; saveSelectedNoteBody (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:512) never touches translateNoteGeneration; and the Edit button (RielaNoteDetailView.swift:212-223) has no .disabled(isTranslateNoteLoading) while the Translate button IS disabled during editing — all as claimed. However, the finding missed that the view's Save handler (RielaNoteDetailView.swift:383-384) calls resetEditingState() right after saveSelectedNoteBody returns, and resetEditingState (line 623) calls clearTranslateNoteState(), which bumps translateNoteGeneration. Since everything is @MainActor and there is no suspension point between the save's completion and the generation bump, the finding's literal scenario ("Save persisted, THEN translation completes, guard passes") is actually blocked. What remains is a narrower but genuine race: if the AI proposeNoteBodyRewrite resumes while the save's client.updateNoteBody is still in flight (save task suspended on its await, generation not yet bumped), the guard at line 29 passes and the translation issues a competing updateNoteBody with the stale pre-edit translation; whichever write commits last on the server wins, so the user's edit can be silently destroyed. Additionally, if the translation completes while the user is mid-edit before tapping Save (startEditing bumps nothing), the translation is silently persisted under the open editor. So: real defect, corrected mechanism/window.

confirmed.title: Body edit draft discarded even when save fails
confirmed.detail: The Save button runs `await viewModel.saveSelectedNoteBody(...)` and then unconditionally calls `resetEditingState()` (RielaNoteDetailView.swift:382-385). `saveSelectedNoteBody` (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:512-532) swallows any thrown error into `state = .failed(...)` and returns normally, so on any update failure (I/O error, store locked, note deleted externally) the draft editor is closed and `bodyDraft.reset()` wipes `draftBodyMarkdown`. The user's entire edit is permanently lost, and RielaNoteDetailView never renders `viewModel.state`, so the only error surface is the note-list overlay which misleadingly says 'Unable to load notes' (RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:95). Same pattern when the `expectedNoteId` guard fails: save silently no-ops and the draft is still discarded.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Make saveSelectedNoteBody report success (return Bool or throw) and only call resetEditingState() on success; keep the editor open with the draft intact and show the error inline in the detail view on failure.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. RielaNoteDetailView.swift:379-385 unconditionally calls resetEditingState() after awaiting saveSelectedNoteBody. saveSelectedNoteBody (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:512-532) returns Void, swallows all thrown errors into state = .failed(...), and silently returns on the expectedNoteId/no-selection guards, so the caller cannot detect failure. resetEditingState() -> bodyDraft.reset() (lines 619-620, 709-713) zeroes draftBodyMarkdown, permanently losing the edit since selectedDetail is not updated on failure. RielaNoteDetailView never renders viewModel.state; the only .failed surface is the note-list overlay 'Unable to load notes' (RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:94-96). Every element of the finding is accurate: a save failure closes the editor, destroys the draft, and shows only a misleading load-error message.

confirmed.title: Compose view dismissed and memo text lost when note creation fails
confirmed.detail: Finding is accurate. Minor imprecision only: the failure feedback shown to the user is whatever the library view renders for state = .failed (error string), not necessarily an 'Unable to load notes' overlay; additionally, even a successful create followed by a failed post-create reload (e.g., loadNotebooksFirstPage throwing) lands in the same .failed state, though in that case the note was actually persisted.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Have the create methods return success/failure; only clear composeDestination/libraryPath on success, otherwise keep the compose view open, reset isSaving, and show the error in the compose UI.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. RielaNoteRootView.swift:195-206 unconditionally sets composeDestination = nil and libraryPath = [.detail] after awaiting the create call; it cannot branch on failure because createUserMemo/createNoteInSelectedNotebook (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:392-452) are non-throwing async Void methods that swallow every error into state = .failed, and createNoteInSelectedNotebook silently returns when selectedNotebookId is nil. RielaNoteComposeView.swift keeps the draft in local @State bodyMarkdown (line 36), which is destroyed when the compose view is dismissed, so a failed create loses the typed markdown with no recovery. isSaving (set true at line 68) is indeed never reset, so the Save button would stay disabled even if the view survived. All load-bearing claims in the finding hold.

confirmed.title: Cmd+Left/Right pager shortcuts stay active while editing the body and silently destroy the draft
confirmed.detail: The note pager buttons carry `.keyboardShortcut(.leftArrow, modifiers: .command)` / `.rightArrow` (lines 237, 252) and the pager is rendered in the header even while `bodyDraft.isEditingBody` is true (header/notePager have no editing check). On macOS, Cmd+Left/Cmd+Right are the standard 'move caret to beginning/end of line' text-editing keys, and SwiftUI key equivalents intercept them before the focused TextEditor. So a user editing a note body who presses Cmd+Left to jump to line start instead triggers selectPreviousNote(); the note switches, `.onChange(of: detail.note.noteId)` (lines 54-57) fires resetEditingState(), and the whole unsaved body draft (plus any in-progress comment/tag text) is silently discarded. The same loss happens by clicking any other note in the list column while editing — there is no unsaved-changes guard anywhere.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Disable the pager buttons (or at least their keyboard shortcuts) while bodyDraft.isEditingBody, and/or prompt to keep-or-discard the draft before switching notes instead of resetting unconditionally in onChange.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. The pager buttons at Sources/RielaNoteUI/RielaNoteDetailView.swift:237/252 carry Cmd+Left/Cmd+Right keyboard shortcuts and are rendered unconditionally in the header (notePager has no bodyDraft.isEditingBody check). On macOS, SwiftUI key equivalents intercept Cmd+arrow before the focused TextEditor's keyDown, so pressing Cmd+Left while editing triggers selectPreviousNote(). selectAdjacentNote/selectNote in RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift have no editing guard (the view model has no reference to the draft state at all), the note switch fires .onChange(of: detail.note.noteId) at lines 54-57, and resetEditingState() (lines 619-625) wipes draftBodyMarkdown, draftTagName, and draftCommentMarkdown with no prompt. The same silent loss occurs when clicking any other note or link while editing. Only trivial caveat: the shortcut is disabled at the first/last note boundary.

confirmed.title: Translate overwrites note body with unvalidated AI output, irreversibly and without review
confirmed.detail: Finding is correct except one sub-point: an empty rewrittenMarkdown does not null the title. NoteService.swift:555 computes `noteTitle(from: bodyMarkdown) ?? existing.title`, so when the new body yields no derivable title the previous title is preserved. Everything else stands: unvalidated AI output (including empty or refusal text) irreversibly overwrites the note body with no prior-version history, no undo, and no user review, unlike the draft-based edit-rewrite flow.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Reject empty/whitespace rewrittenMarkdown (throw invalidOutput); present the translation as a reviewable draft like proposeBodyRewrite, or at minimum preserve the pre-translation body (e.g., as a comment/meta backup) before updateNoteBody.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. translateSelectedNote() (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel+Translation.swift:21-32) persists draft.rewrittenMarkdown via client.updateNoteBody immediately after the workflow returns, with no validation (empty-string check, sanity check against originalMarkdown) and no user confirmation; the UI button (RielaNoteDetailView.swift:178-181) invokes it directly. parseNoteEditRewriteDraft (RielaNoteWorkflowEditRewriteProvider.swift:247) merely decodes RielaNoteEditRewriteDraft, whose non-optional rewrittenMarkdown happily accepts "" or refusal text. NoteService.updateNoteBody (NoteService.swift:542-590) is a plain SQL UPDATE with no revision/history/undo mechanism anywhere in NoteService, so the overwrite is irreversible. The contrast with the draft-based rewrite flow (proposeBodyRewrite returns the draft instead of persisting) is accurate.

confirmed.title: NoteAutoActionTaskLauncher blocks the calling thread with semaphore.wait() for the entire nested workflow run
confirmed.detail: Finding is accurate with two nuances: (1) the blocking behavior is deliberate — Tests/RielaCLITests/NoteAutoActionWorkflowDispatcherTests.swift testDefaultTaskLauncherBlocksCallerUntilOperationCompletes asserts launch() blocks the caller — but deliberate synchronous blocking on a cooperative-pool thread waiting for a Task on the same pool is still the defect; (2) an originatingActionId guard in enqueueAutoActions (AutoActionDispatching.swift:235) prevents auto-action-triggered workflows from re-triggering auto-actions (no unbounded recursion), and dispatch attempts are capped at 3, but neither prevents the first-level blocking dispatch or the pool-exhaustion hang. NoteService.init additionally calls recoverInterruptedAutoActionDispatches() before retryPendingAutoActionDispatches(), re-marking in-flight rows pending, so construction-time replay of up to 50 blocking workflow runs is confirmed as described.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Make dispatch genuinely async (remove the semaphore; have callers that must wait use an async API or a completion-drain at process exit), or run the wait on a dedicated non-cooperative thread; never block cooperative-pool threads on nested workflow runs.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. NoteAutoActionTaskLauncher.launch (NoteAutoActionWorkflowDispatcher.swift:18-25) spawns Task(priority: .background) and immediately semaphore.wait()s until the entire nested WorkflowRunCommand.run completes, making dispatch fully synchronous. It is wired with default launcher into NoteAddonContext (ProductionNodeAdapter+NoteAddons.swift:108-112), and executeNoteAddon is async on a plain struct with no custom executor, so it runs on the Swift cooperative thread pool. NoteService.createNote/createNotebook call dispatchQueuedAutoActions inline, so a note-create add-on whose note matches an enabled auto-action parks its cooperative-pool thread in semaphore.wait() while the inner Task needs the same non-growing pool — with concurrent note-creating nodes >= pool width this is a permanent hang, and even one dispatch blocks the node thread for the nested workflow's full duration. NoteService.init (NoteService.swift:26-29) also calls retryPendingAutoActionDispatches() (limit 50) at construction, synchronously replaying backlogged workflow runs one blocking dispatch at a time. The originatingActionId guard in enqueueAutoActions only prevents recursive auto-action chains, not the first-level dispatch.

confirmed.title: README-documented 'Ask for changes' edit-agent and selection Q&A UI is missing from the detail view (regressed by snapshot commit)
confirmed.detail: README.md lines 104-137 document, as shipped behavior, an 'Ask for changes' agent pill in edit mode, cmd-K selection-scoped rewrites, an 'Ask question shift-cmd-K' selection-question flow with 'Saved as comment' feedback. The current RielaNoteDetailView bodyEditor is a plain TextEditor with only Cancel/Save; grep shows zero UI callers of RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.proposeBodyRewrite, askSelectionQuestion, or RielaNoteSelectableTextEditor anywhere in the repo. Git history confirms a regression: commit 2c9a892 ('Add Riela Note selection Q&A comments feature') contained the 'Ask for changes' UI in RielaNoteDetailView.swift, and commit c91f8dc ('Snapshot working tree: apple-gateway review fixes + concurrent in-flight work') replaced the file with a version lacking all of it (-241 net lines across RielaNoteUI). The view-model layer (LibraryViewModel+EditRewrite/+SelectionQA), helpers (rielaNoteApplyingRewrite, rielaNoteSelectionQACommentMarkdown), NSViewRepresentable editor, workflow providers (wired in Sources/RielaApp/NoteWindowController.swift:36-43) and 1100+ lines of tests all still exist but are unreachable. A user following the README presses Edit and finds no agent pill and no cmd-K affordance.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Restore the edit-agent UI from commit 2c9a892's RielaNoteDetailView.swift (header ask-for-changes pill, RielaNoteSelectableTextEditor with selection chips, question mode) and reconcile it with the translation control added in c91f8dc; alternatively, if the removal was intentional, update README and delete the dead VM/provider/editor code.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code and git history. README.md (~lines 104-137) documents the 'Ask for changes' edit-agent pill, cmd-K selection rewrites, and shift-cmd-K selection-question flow as shipped behavior, but the current Sources/RielaNoteUI/RielaNoteDetailView.swift bodyEditor is a plain TextEditor with only Cancel/Save. Grep shows zero UI callers of proposeBodyRewrite, askSelectionQuestion, or RielaNoteSelectableTextEditor (only the view-model extensions and tests reference them). Git history confirms the regression exactly as described: commit 2c9a892's RielaNoteDetailView.swift contained the full UI (verified at lines 217, 420, 429, 750, 783 of that revision), and snapshot commit c91f8dc replaced the file (42 insertions / 234 deletions), removing it; no later commits touched the file. NoteWindowController.swift:36-43 still wires editRewriteProvider and selectionQuestionProvider, which are now unreachable dead wiring. The snapshot commit message even states it mixed concurrent in-flight work and was 'not intended to be merged wholesale', supporting the accidental-clobber interpretation.

confirmed.title: Failed note-body save silently destroys the user's edit draft
confirmed.detail: The Save button runs `await viewModel.saveSelectedNoteBody(draftBodyMarkdown, expectedNoteId:)` then unconditionally calls `resetEditingState()` (lines 379-386), which clears bodyDraft. On any save failure (DB error, note made read-only via CLI, store locked by another process), saveSelectedNoteBody (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:512-532) only sets `state = .failed`; the view then wipes the draft and closes the editor, reverting to the old body. The only error surface is the notebook-list overlay ('Unable to load notes'), which in compact (NavigationStack) layout is on a different screen entirely, so the user sees their typed edits vanish with zero feedback. Long manual edits are unrecoverable.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Make saveSelectedNoteBody return success/failure; only reset the editing state on success, keep the editor open with the draft and show an inline error near the Save button on failure.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. RielaNoteDetailView.swift:379-385 unconditionally calls resetEditingState() after awaiting saveSelectedNoteBody, which returns Void and does not throw; on failure RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:529-531 only sets state = .failed and leaves selectedDetail with the old body. resetEditingState() -> bodyDraft.reset() (lines 709-713) clears draftBodyMarkdown and closes the editor, so the user's edits vanish and the stale body is shown. The detail view never reads viewModel.state (grep confirms zero references), and the only .failed rendering in the module is the 'Unable to load notes' overlay in RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:94-95, which in compact layout is a separate NavigationStack screen behind the pushed detail view. Failure paths are real: client.updateNoteBody is try await (store errors, concurrent read-only change after the stale !readOnly check at line 262). Tests only cover success and expectedNoteId-mismatch cases, not save failure.

confirmed.title: Compose view dismisses and discards memo text when creation fails
confirmed.detail: composeView's onSave runs createUserMemo/createNoteInSelectedNotebook and then unconditionally sets `composeDestination = nil; libraryPath = [.detail]` (lines 195-205). Both view-model methods swallow errors into `state = .failed` (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:392-452). If the create fails (DB locked by a concurrent CLI run, disk error), the compose editor is torn down and the typed memo body is lost; the user lands on the detail screen of an unrelated note, and the only feedback is a misleading 'Unable to load notes' overlay on the list. RielaNoteComposeView also sets `isSaving = true` before calling onSave and never resets it, so even if the compose view were kept alive the Save button would stay permanently disabled.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Have createUserMemo/createNoteInSelectedNotebook report success; keep the compose view (with its text) open on failure, reset isSaving, and show an inline error.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code: onSave unconditionally dismisses compose after create calls that swallow errors into state=.failed, losing the typed memo text; the 'Unable to load notes' overlay and never-reset isSaving flag are also as described.

confirmed.title: Notes window creates NoteService without an autoActionDispatcher, so default AI auto-tagging and all configured auto-actions never fire for app-created or app-edited notes
confirmed.detail: Finding is accurate as written. Minor addition: the same dispatcherless construction also exists at Sources/RielaApp/NoteSettingsWindowController.swift:135, so the settings-window service has the same gap.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Wire a NoteAutoActionWorkflowDispatcher (or an app-side equivalent using the resolved riela executable) into the NoteService created by NoteWindowController, or at minimum enqueue dispatches without a dispatcher so a later CLI run can retry them, and surface in the Config tab when no dispatcher is active.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. NoteWindowController.swift:31 builds NoteService(driver:) with the default nil autoActionDispatcher, and nothing in RielaApp/RielaNoteUI ever sets one (the dispatcher type NoteAutoActionWorkflowDispatcher exists only in the RielaCLI target, which RielaApp does not depend on per Package.swift). NoteService.enqueueAutoActions (AutoActionDispatching.swift:231-236) guards on autoActionDispatcher != nil and returns [] when nil, so dispatch rows are never inserted and no later retry can recover them. NoteStoreSchema.seedAutoActions (NoteStoreSchema.swift:77-94) seeds enabled default-ai-tagging actions for note-created/note-updated, and the design doc (riela-note-design.md line 9) states AI adds tags on note creation/edit by default. The CLI path (NoteCommands.swift:582-590) wires the dispatcher, so the CLI/app behavioral divergence described is real. The app's UI client (RielaNoteUIClient.swift:624) also exposes configureAutoAction on the same dispatcherless service, so user-configured auto-actions from the app can never fire on app-side note events.

confirmed.title: Notebook-scoped listNotes overload orders by created_at DESC, returning bulk-ingested notebook pages in random order
confirmed.detail: The listNotes(limit:offset:notebookId:tagFilter:) overload orders by `created_at DESC, note_id` even when notebookId is given, while the other overload (line 485) correctly orders by note_number. insertNotebookWithNotes (NoteService.swift:233) stamps ONE `now` value on every page of an ingested notebook, so all notes tie on created_at and the tie-break falls to note_id = "note-<ms>-<uuid>": notes inserted in the same millisecond sort by random UUID. Concrete failure: import a 10-page document via createNotebookWithNotes, then query GraphQL `notes(notebookId: ...)` (Sources/RielaGraphQL/NoteGraphQLService.swift:63 routes to this overload) - pages come back shuffled (and even with distinct timestamps, newest-first instead of page order), so any GraphQL/CLI/agent consumer renders the document out of order. The SwiftUI client happens to use the note_number overload, masking the bug in the app UI only.
confirmed.fix_sketch: When notebookId is provided, order by note_number, note_id (matching the other overload), or delegate to it; keep created_at DESC only for the cross-notebook feed case.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. The generic listNotes overload (NoteService.swift:494-539) orders by `created_at DESC, note_id` (line 532) even when notebookId is supplied, while the notebook-scoped overload (line 485) orders by `note_number, note_id`. insertNotebookWithNotes (line 233) stamps one `now` value on every page's created_at (lines 279-280), so all pages of a bulk-ingested notebook tie on created_at and ordering falls to note_id, which is "note-<epochMillis>-<random UUID>" (NoteService+Rows.swift:163-166) — same-millisecond pages sort by random UUID. GraphQL notes(notebookId:) (NoteGraphQLService.swift:63-68) routes to this overload with no re-sorting, so GraphQL consumers get notebook pages out of document order. The failure scenario is real; the note_number overload used by the SwiftUI client masks it only in the app UI.

confirmed.title: updateNoteBody silently overwrites explicit user-set titles with a body-derived title
confirmed.detail: The finding is factually correct but overstates unintentionality: the exact scenario (explicit title replaced by first line of edited body) is deliberately asserted by testUpdateNoteBodyDerivesTitleFromFirstLineWhenUpdatedBodyHasNoHeading (Tests/RielaNoteTests/NoteServiceTests.swift:38-46), so this is encoded current behavior, not an overlooked code path. Also, the built-in RielaNoteUI never sets explicit titles (compose view derives titles; RielaNoteUIClient.createNote has no title parameter), so the loss only affects notes created with an explicit title via GraphQL CreateNoteInput.title or the library API. The substantive defect stands: the API accepts explicit titles at creation but any subsequent body edit (including via the note UI) irrecoverably replaces them, and there is no title-update API to restore them. Fixing it requires either persisting a title-provenance flag (explicit vs derived) or adding an update-title API; the existing test at NoteServiceTests.swift:38-46 will need updating alongside any fix.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Track whether the title was explicitly set (e.g. meta flag or compare stored title against derivation of the old body); only re-derive when the current title was itself derived, or add a title parameter/mutation.
confirmed.verify_note: All cited mechanics verified by reading the code: NoteService.swift:555 recomputes the title on every body update (noteTitle(from:) ?? existing.title); NoteTitleDerivation falls back to the first non-empty non-fence line so derivation succeeds for virtually any non-blank body; createNote accepts an explicit title (NoteService.swift:87/120) exposed via GraphQL (NoteGraphQLService.swift:136); no updateNoteTitle/rename API exists anywhere, so a clobbered explicit title is unrecoverable; and the UI edit path (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:521) routes through updateNoteBody. The failure scenario is real. It is not harmless: silent, unrecoverable loss of user-supplied data for GraphQL/library clients.

confirmed.title: Local-blob delete failure after DB commit misreports a successful migration as a failure and permanently orphans the local file
confirmed.detail: Finding is accurate as written. One minor refinement: LocalNoteFileStore.delete checks fileExists before removeItem, so a missing local blob does not throw; the unhandled throws are limited to actual removeItem failures (permission change, immutable flag, file lock, filesystem error), which are exactly the cases the finding cites. No data loss occurs (S3 object and DB record are consistent); the defect is the false failure report plus a permanently orphaned local blob with no cleanup or retry path.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Treat post-commit local deletion as best-effort: catch the delete error, still return the migrated record, and surface the leftover path as a warning (e.g., a `cleanupFailures` list on NoteFileMigrationResult) instead of throwing.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. NoteFileMigration.swift commits the DB transaction (storage_kind='s3', local_path=NULL) at lines 56-83, then line 84 calls `try localStore.delete(record: original)` with no error handling. LocalNoteFileStore.delete (LocalNoteFileStore.swift:95-106) tolerates a missing file but uses a plain `try FileManager.default.removeItem(at:)`, which throws on permission/immutable/filesystem errors. When it throws, migrateFileStorage throws after the migration is durable; migrateAllLocalFiles (lines 104-118) records the fileId in `failures` and omits it from `migrated`, misreporting a committed migration as failed (the GraphQL mutation path in NoteGraphQLDocumentExecutor.swift:275 misreports the same way). A rerun selects only WHERE storage_kind='local' (line 98), so the file is never re-selected, and no orphan/cleanup/reconcile code exists anywhere in Sources/RielaNote, so the leftover blob under files/<shard>/<fileId> is permanently orphaned. Every claim in the finding matches the code; the only trivial overstatement is that delete does tolerate an already-missing file, which does not affect the failure scenario.

confirmed.title: No reclamation path for file blobs: deleting notes/notebooks orphans files rows and on-disk/S3 blobs forever
confirmed.detail: deleteNoteRows deletes only the note_files join rows (line 903) and deleteNotebook only notebook_files (line 639); nothing in the codebase ever executes DELETE FROM files or removes a stored blob outside migration/attach-compensation (verified via grep: zero `DELETE FROM files` hits, no detach/deleteFile API, no GC/sweep). Every attachment made via attachFile/attachNotebookFile therefore consumes disk (or S3) space permanently: a user who attaches large files and then deletes the notes reclaims nothing, and there is no API to reclaim it manually either. Note: testFileRecordAndContentSurviveNoteDeletion (Tests/RielaNoteTests/NoteFileStoreTests.swift:136) pins that records survive note deletion, so record survival itself is intentional (likely for sharing), but the complete absence of any refcount/GC/delete path makes storage growth unbounded in normal use.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Add a garbage-collection pass (delete files rows with no note_files/notebook_files references, then delete the corresponding local/S3 blobs) exposed as a service method, and/or a deleteFile/detachFile API; keep the existing survive-note-deletion semantics for still-referenced files.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. deleteNoteRows (NoteService.swift:903) removes only note_files join rows and deleteNotebook (line 639) only notebook_files; grep confirms zero DELETE FROM files statements in Sources. NoteFileStore.delete(record:) exists (Local and S3 implementations) but is invoked only from attach-failure compensation blocks (NoteService+Files.swift:49,103,159,206,253) and S3 migration cleanup (NoteFileMigration.swift:84); there is no public detach/delete-file API. insertFileRecord always creates a fresh fileId with no sha256 dedup and no attach-existing-file API, so blobs cannot even be shared across notes, yet they survive all deletions forever. Test NoteFileStoreTests.swift:136 pins record survival as intentional, but the complete absence of any refcount/GC/manual-delete path makes storage growth unbounded, exactly as the finding states.

confirmed.title: LibSQL local driver opens a fresh unserialized connection per withDatabase call, breaking drop-in parity with the SQLite driver
confirmed.detail: withDatabase(.local) calls SQLiteDatabase.open on every invocation with no caching and no serialization, unlike SQLiteNoteDatabaseDriver which routes all access through one NSLock-guarded cached connection. Two concrete consequences: (1) Concurrency: NoteService is Sendable and shared; two concurrent write operations (e.g. attachFile and upsertAutoAction) through one LibSQLNoteDatabaseDriver each get an independent connection, so overlapping BEGIN IMMEDIATE transactions contend at the SQLite level - if one write transaction outlasts the 3s busy_timeout (SQLiteOpenOptions default 3000ms), the other throws SQLiteError 'database is locked', an error the interchangeable SQLite driver never produces within one driver instance. (2) Overhead-as-behavior: the note drivers default requireFTS5=true and requireJSONB=true, so EVERY note operation re-runs PRAGMA foreign_keys, PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL, the JSONB probe query, and a CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE temp.riela_fts5_probe_<uuid> USING fts5 + DROP (SQLiteDatabase.configure -> runFTS5Probe), i.e. real DDL executed per call; a NoteService flow like attachFile issues two withDatabase calls and thus two full open+probe cycles per attach. Currently only env-gated tests (Tests/RielaNoteTests/NoteStoreSchemaTests.swift:247) construct this driver, but it is an exported library product (Package.swift line 24) positioned as a drop-in NoteDatabaseDriving implementation.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Reuse the cached-connection pattern: make SQLiteNoteDatabaseConnection (NoteDatabaseDriving.swift) internal-visible or duplicate it in RielaNoteLibSQL, and have LibSQLNoteDatabaseDriver hold one lock-guarded lazily-opened connection for the .local case instead of opening per call.
confirmed.verify_note: Verified at Sources/RielaNoteLibSQL/LibSQLNoteDatabaseDriver.swift:133 — withDatabase(.local) opens a fresh SQLiteDatabase per call with no caching or locking, while SQLiteNoteDatabaseDriver (Sources/RielaNote/NoteDatabaseDriving.swift:41-61) serializes all access through one NSLock-guarded cached connection. NoteDatabaseDriving and NoteService are Sendable, so concurrent use through one LibSQL driver instance yields independent connections whose BEGIN IMMEDIATE transactions (SQLiteDatabase.transaction default) contend at the SQLite level with the default 3000ms busy timeout, producing 'database is locked' errors the SQLite driver cannot produce intra-instance. The per-call overhead claim is also accurate: every open runs configure() — PRAGMA foreign_keys, PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL, the JSONB probe query, and the FTS5 CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE + DROP probe (note drivers default requireFTS5=true, requireJSONB=true) — and attachFile issues two withDatabase calls per invocation. RielaNoteLibSQL is an exported library product (Package.swift line 24); no guard prevents the failure scenario.

confirmed.title: migrateAllNoteFiles leaks raw internal error text in ControlPlaneResult.diagnostics despite redaction elsewhere
confirmed.detail: noteFileMigrationControlResult(_:) builds diagnostics as "\(fileId): \(message)" where message is `String(describing: error)` captured in NoteFileMigration.swift:115. Those errors come from local file reads and S3 store operations and can contain absolute local paths, S3 endpoint/bucket/key details, and HTTP error internals. The same executor deliberately redacts the very same failures in the `failures` list to the constant "note file migration failed" (line 315), and single-file migrateNoteFileStorage redacts via graphQLNotePublicDiagnostic. So when any file fails during migrateAllNoteFiles, the GraphQL response's result.diagnostics exposes unredacted server internals to the API client, contradicting the established public-diagnostic policy.
confirmed.fix_sketch: In noteFileMigrationControlResult(_:), map each failure through the same redaction used for the failures array (e.g. "\(fileId): note file migration failed") or pass failures through graphQLNotePublicDiagnostic.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. NoteFileMigration.swift:115 stores String(describing: error) as the failure message for each file that fails in migrateAllLocalFiles; those errors include Data(contentsOf:) NSErrors (absolute local paths), NoteFileStoreError.invalidEndpoint carrying profile.endpoint.absoluteString, and rethrown URLSession transport errors. NoteGraphQLDocumentExecutor.swift:702 then emits "\(fileId): \(message)" into ControlPlaneResult.diagnostics, a client-selectable GraphQL field (selection map lines 545-549). This contradicts the redaction applied in the same handler: the parallel failures list is hardcoded to "note file migration failed" (line 315), and single-file migration plus all other error paths route through graphQLNotePublicDiagnostic, which collapses unknown errors to "note operation failed". No guard sanitizes diagnostics before encoding, so the leak is reachable on any per-file migration failure.

confirmed.title: Silent limit clamping (max 200, min 1) breaks client pagination and causes silent data omission
confirmed.detail: Finding is accurate as written. Minor nuance: the clamp is a deliberate defensive cap (explicit `maximum: 200` parameter), so the defect is specifically the undocumented/silent nature of it — the schema advertises unbounded `limit: Int` and the response payloads expose no pagination metadata (no totalCount/hasMore), making the clamp undetectable to clients.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Either reject limits > 200 / < 0 with an invalidVariable error, or document the max in the schema contract and surface the effective limit; honor limit: 0 by returning an empty list.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code: boundedLimit (NoteGraphQLDocumentExecutor.swift:785-787) silently clamps limit to [1,200] for notes/notebooks/searchNotes/proposeNoteLinks; the schema (GraphQLContracts.swift:919-923, GraphQLNoteSchemaContract.swift) declares plain `limit: Int` with no documented maximum; and the payload types (e.g. NotesQueryPayload { result, value: [Note!] }) carry no totalCount/hasMore/nextOffset, so a client cannot detect the clamp. A client requesting limit: 500 against a >200-row collection receives 200 rows, and the standard "returned < requested" termination heuristic concludes exhaustion, permanently omitting the rest. limit: 0 → 1 is also accurate. The cap is intentional, but the silent undocumented contract mismatch and undetectable data omission are a real defect, not a style opinion.

confirmed.title: Schema advertises a multi-field Query/Mutation type but executor rejects any document with more than one root selection
confirmed.detail: Finding is accurate as stated. One refinement: the count guard fires before the supportedNoteGraphQLFields check, so multi-root documents mixing note and control-plane fields also get the executor-generated error rather than falling through as unhandled; in the served configuration either path yields a 400, so all multi-root documents against the advertised schema fail.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Either execute multiple root selections (loop over parsed fields, merge into one data object) or document the single-root-selection restriction in the schema contract text returned to clients.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. NoteGraphQLDocumentParsing.swift:44-48 throws for any document with >1 root selection; NoteGraphQLDocumentExecutor.execute (NoteGraphQLDocumentExecutor.swift:75-93) converts that throw into a handled GraphQL error response (not .notHandled), and this executor is the sole GraphQLDocumentExecuting conformance, wired as the only graphQLExecutor on the served /graphql route (WorkflowServingController.swift:476, ServerContracts.swift:215-226). Meanwhile the published SDL (GraphQLContracts.swift:916-964 and GraphQLNoteSchemaContract.swift, exposed via ScopedParityCommands.swift:136 and ServerContracts.swift:234) advertises multi-field Query/Mutation types with no mention of the one-root-selection restriction. So spec-valid documents like `{ tags {...} tagClasses {...} }` or two aliased note() lookups validate against the advertised schema but fail at runtime. A regression test (NoteGraphQLDocumentParsingRegressionTests.swift:194) shows the restriction is intentional, but intentional-and-undeclared contract divergence that breaks schema-driven clients is a real defect, not harmless.

confirmed.title: authenticate() leaks raw internal error details to unauthenticated callers in 401 body
confirmed.detail: The catch branch of authenticate() returns .rejected(noteAPIUnauthorizedResponse("\(error)")), interpolating the raw thrown error into the response body sent to a caller who has NOT proven any identity. service.authenticateAPIClient() runs SQLite queries (Sources/RielaNote/NoteService+APIClients.swift:59-83); on failure it throws SQLiteError, whose Equatable struct interpolation includes `message` (e.g. "failed to open sqlite database at /Users/<user>/.riela/note/...: <sqlite msg>" from SQLiteDatabase.swift:149) and the raw `sql` text ("SELECT client_id, display_name, token_hash ... FROM api_clients WHERE token_hash = ?"). Concrete scenario: the note DB file becomes unreadable (disk full, permissions, corruption) while the note API is served; any remote unauthenticated client POSTing to /graphql with a note query and any Authorization header receives a 401 whose body discloses the operator's absolute filesystem paths and the api_clients table schema. This contradicts the codebase's own sanitization convention (graphQLNotePublicDiagnostic in NoteGraphQLService.swift:470 maps unknown errors to "note operation failed").
confirmed.fix_sketch: Replace the interpolated error with a fixed message, e.g. .rejected(noteAPIUnavailableResponse("note API authentication is temporarily unavailable")) (or a generic 401), and log the underlying error via telemetry instead of the response body.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. QRClientRegistrationAuthenticator.authenticate() line 160 interpolates the raw thrown error into noteAPIUnauthorizedResponse, whose 401 body (error + graphql.errors[0].message) is returned directly to the caller by ServerContracts.swift:205-209 before any identity is proven (any non-empty bearer token reaches the throwing path). service.authenticateAPIClient (NoteService+APIClients.swift:59-83) throws SQLiteError, which has no CustomStringConvertible, so "\(error)" reflectively dumps the struct including message ("failed to open sqlite database at <absolute path>: ..." from SQLiteDatabase.swift:149) and sql (full SELECT/UPDATE text with api_clients schema, attached at SQLiteDatabase.swift:193/208/224). The lazy connection in NoteDatabaseDriving.swift:52-59 means open failures surface at request time, and query-time I/O/corruption errors can occur anytime even after the connection is cached. The sanitization convention graphQLNotePublicDiagnostic (NoteGraphQLService.swift:470) confirms this leak contradicts the codebase's established pattern.

confirmed.title: POST /note/register 500 handler echoes raw internal errors to unauthenticated clients
confirmed.detail: One overstatement: the leak is not reachable by "any network client that submits a valid-format registration body". redeemRegistrationCode(code:displayName:) validates the code against the in-memory challenge store (lines 129-134) before touching SQLite; clients without a valid, unexpired registration code get sanitized 404/410 responses and never trigger the DB write. The raw-error 500 is only reachable by a client holding a live registration code — still pre-authentication (no bearer token yet, codes distributed via QR), so the disclosure to a not-yet-trusted client stands, just with a narrower trigger population. Related but separate: authenticate() at line 160 echoes "\(error)" in 401 rejections to fully anonymous clients when a DB read fails, which is the same leak pattern with a wider audience.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Return a fixed message ("registration failed") for the generic catch and record the underlying error via telemetry/logging.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. QRClientRegistrationAuthenticator.swift:122-124 returns status 500 with body ["error": .string("\(error)")] for any non-NoteAPIRegistrationError. SQLiteError (Sources/RielaSQLite/SQLiteDatabase.swift:47-59) is a plain struct whose default interpolation dumps all fields, including message (prefixed with the database's absolute path by messageWithPath, line 358-363) and sql (the raw INSERT INTO api_clients statement, attached at lines 193/200 via sqliteError(operation:sql:)). service.registerAPIClient (NoteService+APIClients.swift:7-29) throws exactly these errors on write failure (read-only volume, disk full), and NoteServiceError messages are also echoed raw. POST /note/register is routed with no authentication (ServerContracts.swift:117-118 -> routeNoteRegistration at 254-264, only a protocol-conformance check). The information-disclosure defect is real.

confirmed.title: Authenticated client identity is trivially spoofable via client-supplied assignedBy/author fields
confirmed.detail: noteAPIAssignedBy(explicit:request:) returns `explicit ?? request.authenticatedClientId.map { "client:\($0)" }`, i.e. the request-body value always wins over the server-verified identity. The same pattern applies to addNoteComment author (line 232). Concrete scenario: registered client A (bearer-authenticated as client:client-A) sends `mutation { applyNoteTags(input: { noteId: "...", tags: [...], assignedBy: "client:client-B", provenance: "human" }) }`; the tag assignment is persisted and displayed as made by client B (or by a human), so the assignedBy/author audit attribution that the whole authenticatedClientId plumbing (ServerContracts.swift:221) exists to provide is untrustworthy whenever more than one client is registered — including forging "human" provenance for AI-applied tags via the client-controlled provenance field. NoteAPIAuthTests.swift:53 only asserts the default (unspecified) case, not the override.
confirmed.fix_sketch: When request.authenticatedClientId is non-nil (HTTP note API path), ignore or reject client-supplied assignedBy/author values (or record both claimed and verified identities); keep the explicit override only for local CLI/operator invocations where authenticatedClientId is nil.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. NoteGraphQLDocumentExecutor.swift:325-327 is exactly `explicit ?? request.authenticatedClientId.map { "client:\($0)" }`, so a client-supplied assignedBy always overrides the bearer-token-verified identity; the same applies to addNoteComment's author at line 232 (`input.author ?? noteAPIAssignedBy(nil, ...) ?? "user"`). ServerContracts.swift:199-221 authenticates the token and threads authenticatedClientId in, but no guard anywhere (executor, NoteGraphQLService.applyTags/addComment, or persistence) validates the explicit value against it. The GraphQL schema exposes assignedBy on ApplyNoteTagsInput/ApplyNotebookTagsInput/CreateNoteInput/SaveNoteConversationInput and provenance is also client-controlled (default "ai" for applyNoteTags), so an authenticated client A can persist tags attributed to "client:client-B" with provenance "human" exactly as described. NoteAPIAuthTests.swift:53 only asserts the fallback (unspecified) case, and its assertion that assignedBy equals "client:<clientId>" shows the field is meant as trusted attribution, which the override defeats. Minor nuance: assignedBy doubles as a freeform label elsewhere (scaffold default "note-config-agent"), so some explicit self-labeling may be intended, but nothing prevents impersonating another client's "client:<id>" form or forging human provenance.

confirmed.title: loadMoreSearchResults appends a stale query's page into the current search results
confirmed.detail: loadMoreSearchResults reads searchText/filters at entry but performs no searchGeneration/isCurrentSearch check after the await. updateSearchText fires performSearch on every keystroke, so this is easy to hit: tap 'Load more' for query X, type a character (query Y replaces searchResults and resets searchResultsOffset), then X's page returns and is appended into Y's results, searchResultsOffset is advanced past Y's real count, and hasMoreSearchResults is set from X's page — the list shows results that do not match the query and further pagination skips or falsely ends. Double-invocation also double-increments searchResultsOffset, silently skipping a page (dedup filter masks it).
confirmed.fix_sketch: Capture the search generation + normalized query/filters at entry and guard all writes after the await with isCurrentSearch; add a reentrancy flag.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. loadMoreSearchResults (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:610-634) has no isCurrentSearch/searchGeneration re-check after its await, unlike performSearch (line 314) and refresh (line 224) which do guard. The class is @MainActor but the await is a suspension point, so a keystroke-driven performSearch (wired per-keystroke via .onChange(of: viewModel.searchText) in RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:35 and RielaNoteFilterPane.swift:69) can replace searchResults and reset searchResultsOffset while the load-more fetch for the old query is in flight; when it returns, its page is appended into the new query's results, searchResultsOffset is advanced past the new list's real count, and hasMoreSearchResults is set from the stale page. The double-invocation claim also holds: canLoadMoreSearchResults is only checked at entry, so two concurrent calls fetch the same offset and double-increment searchResultsOffset, with the noteId dedup filter masking the duplicates while a page is skipped.

confirmed.title: load() clears search state without a generation guard, wiping results of a search started meanwhile
confirmed.detail: performSearch with an empty query awaits load(), and load() unconditionally sets searchResults = [], searchResultsOffset = 0, hasMoreSearchResults = false after several awaits, with no searchGeneration check. Scenario: user deletes the last character of the query (empty text triggers advanceSearchGeneration + load()), then quickly types a new query; the new performSearch fetches and applies its results (its generation is current), but the still-running load() then resumes and clears searchResults — the user sees 'no results' for a query that has matches. load() may also then run selectNotebook(first) and change the selection under the new search.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Have load() capture the search generation when invoked from performSearch and skip the search-state reset (and first-notebook autoselect) if a newer search generation exists.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. load() (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:187-203) unconditionally clears searchResults/searchResultsOffset/hasMoreSearchResults at lines 193-195 after three awaits, with no searchGeneration capture or check, and may then call selectNotebook(first). performSearch with an empty query advances the generation and awaits load() (lines 304-305). Callers spawn a separate unstructured Task per keystroke (RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:35-38, RielaNoteFilterPane.swift:67-71) with no cancellation/debounce, so @MainActor reentrancy lets a newer search fetch and apply its results (guarded by isCurrentSearch in its own task) while the stale load() is suspended; when load() resumes it wipes the fresh results even though isSearching is true, showing an empty list for a matching query. The omission is clearly a defect rather than intent: refresh() (lines 210, 224-232) and the selection paths capture and check generations before mutating the same state, while load() does not.

confirmed.title: Mutation flows assign selectedDetail after await without a selection-generation guard, snapping the UI back to the old note
confirmed.detail: Finding is correct with two small precisions: (1) applyTagToSelectedNote and removeTagFromSelectedNote assign selectedDetail via replaceSelectedDetail(_:) (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:787) rather than directly, but that helper is equally unguarded; (2) only saveSelectedNoteBody resets contentMode and calls clearResolvedFileSelection (526-527) — addComment/tag/link mutations overwrite selectedDetail only. The core defect (post-await selectedDetail writes without selection-generation checks, and create flows not advancing selectionGeneration so watcher-triggered refresh can overwrite the new memo's selection) is verified.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Advance selectionGeneration at the start of each mutation targeting the selected note and guard the post-await selectedDetail/state writes with isCurrentSelection; have create flows advance it too.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. All six cited mutation paths (saveSelectedNoteBody:521, applyTag:550 and removeTag:565 via unguarded replaceSelectedDetail:787-792, addComment:582, linkSelectedNote:883, acceptLinkProposal LinkProposals.swift:50) write selectedDetail after an await without checking isCurrentSelection or advancing selectionGeneration, while selectNote/selectNotebook/refresh are generation-guarded. Note rows call selectNote with no loading-state gating (RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:191), so selecting note B during note A's in-flight mutation is reachable, and A's late response overwrites B's selectedDetail (save additionally clears resolved-file state and forces contentMode=.text). The secondary scenario is also real: RielaNoteRootView.swift:226-239 wires a store-change watcher that calls refresh() on the app's own DB writes; createUserMemo/createNoteInSelectedNotebook (392/421) never advance selectionGeneration, so a concurrently started refresh passes its stale-generation guards (lines 243/255) and line 259 restores the previously selected note over the freshly created memo.

confirmed.title: submitDraft has no reentrancy guard; concurrent first turns split one conversation into two notebooks
confirmed.detail: canSubmit (state != .loading) only gates the Send button; RielaNoteAgentView's TextField .onSubmit (RielaNoteAgentView.swift:87-91) calls submitDraft directly, and submitDraft itself only checks that the message is non-empty. Scenario: user sends message 1 (slow answerNoteAgentTurn), types message 2 and presses Return while loading; two submitDraft calls interleave, both see autoSaveNotebookId == nil in saveOrAppend, and both call saveNoteAgentConversation — creating two separate notebooks for one conversation, with autoSaveNotebookId ending up as whichever finished last, so subsequent turns append to only one of them (persisted transcript permanently split). Turn order in `turns` can also invert relative to send order.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Guard submitDraft with `guard state != .loading` (or an isSubmitting flag) and queue/reject submissions while a turn is in flight.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. submitDraft (RielaNoteAgentViewModel.swift:46-50) guards only on a non-empty trimmed message; the canSubmit computed property (state != .loading) is applied only via .disabled on the Send Button (RielaNoteAgentView.swift:104), while the TextField's .onSubmit (lines 87-91) calls submitDraft unconditionally and the field is never disabled during loading. Because the @MainActor method suspends at `await client.answerNoteAgentTurn` (line 54) and inside saveOrAppend (lines 108-115), a second Return-key submission interleaves with the first; on a first turn, autoSaveNotebookId is only assigned at line 113 after saveNoteAgentConversation returns, so both interleaved calls can observe autoSaveNotebookId == nil in saveOrAppend and each call saveNoteAgentConversation, producing two notebooks for one conversation. autoSaveNotebookId retains whichever save completed last, so later turns append to only that notebook (persisted transcript permanently split), and turns.append at line 59 orders by completion, so turn order can invert relative to send order. No tests or other guards mitigate this.

confirmed.title: Comment draft cleared even when adding the comment fails
confirmed.detail: Finding is essentially correct. Minor nuance: the error is not entirely invisible — viewModel.state = .failed is rendered as a banner in RielaNoteNotebookListView (line 94), a separate pane, but nothing is shown in the detail view itself and the draft comment/tag text is still unconditionally cleared on failure.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Only clear the draft when the mutation succeeded (return Bool from the view-model methods) and surface the error next to the comment/tag editor.
confirmed.verify_note: Verified in code: the Add comment button (Sources/RielaNoteUI/RielaNoteDetailView.swift ~525-530) unconditionally sets draftCommentMarkdown = "" after awaiting addCommentToSelectedNote, which is a Void async method that catches all errors and only sets state = .failed (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift ~572-587). No guard prevents failure, so a failed client.addComment call erases the typed draft. The same unconditional-clear pattern exists for draftTagName after applyTagToSelectedNote, which also swallows errors. The .failed state is only rendered in RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:94, not in the detail view, so the detail view gives no feedback.

confirmed.title: Add-link sheet dismisses with implied success even when linking fails
confirmed.detail: The 'Add link' button runs `await viewModel.linkSelectedNote(to:kind:)` then calls `onLinked()` unconditionally (lines 57-62). linkSelectedNote (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:872-892) catches all errors into `state = .failed`, so if the link mutation fails (e.g., target note deleted between search and click, store error) the sheet closes as if the link were created; no link appears in the Links section and the only feedback is the unrelated 'Unable to load notes' overlay in the list column. User reasonably believes the link was added.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Have linkSelectedNote report success; keep the sheet open and show the error text (the sheet already has an errorText state) when the link fails, calling onLinked() only on success.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. RielaNoteLinkSearchSheet.swift:54-62 awaits viewModel.linkSelectedNote(to:kind:) and then calls onLinked() unconditionally; linkSelectedNote (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:872-892) is non-throwing/Void and swallows all errors into state = .failed. onLinked (RielaNoteDetailView.swift:63) sets isAddLinkPresented = false, dismissing the sheet, so a failed link mutation (target deleted between search and click, store error) closes the sheet as if it succeeded with no link created. The only feedback is the failed library state rendered as the unrelated 'Unable to load notes' ContentUnavailableView in RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:94-95, which replaces the note list. No guard prevents the scenario, and the sibling RielaNoteLinkProposalSheet in the same file shows the inline-error pattern this sheet lacks.

confirmed.title: Full markdown re-parse on every parent body evaluation makes typing laggy on huge notes
confirmed.detail: Finding is essentially correct. Two small refinements: (1) the per-block AttributedString markdown conversion is NOT repeated per keystroke, because blocks is Equatable and SwiftUI can skip RielaNoteMarkdownBodyView.body when the parsed blocks are unchanged — the repeated cost is exactly the block-level parse (newline split + per-line trims + regexes) in init; (2) comment bodies are re-parsed even when the Comments DisclosureGroup is collapsed, since DisclosureGroup builds its content eagerly, making the finding slightly broader than stated.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Cache parsed blocks keyed by the markdown string (e.g., compute blocks in body via a memoized store, or make RielaNoteMarkdownBodyView hold `let markdown` and parse inside an Equatable child view / onChange-cached @State) so re-render with identical markdown skips parsing.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. RielaNoteMarkdownBlock.parse runs eagerly in RielaNoteMarkdownBodyView.init (RielaNoteMarkdownBodyView.swift:6-8), and the view is constructed at RielaNoteDetailView.swift:267 (note body) and :503 (each comment) inside eagerly-evaluated ViewBuilder closures (ScrollView/VStack/DisclosureGroup all invoke content in init). draftCommentMarkdown and draftTagName are @State on RielaNoteDetailView, so each keystroke in the comment TextEditor (line 518) or tag TextField (line 563) re-evaluates the whole detail body, re-running the line-splitting/regex parser over the full note markdown and all comment markdown on the main thread. No caching, memoization, or guard prevents this in preview mode; the isEditingBody guard only applies while editing the note body itself.

confirmed.title: Link-proposal provider silently ignores RIELA_NOTE_LINK_EXTRACT_WORKFLOW_DIR while sibling providers honor their dir env vars
confirmed.detail: defaultProvider's allowEnvironmentOverrides defaults to false here (true for the edit-rewrite and selection-question providers), and the app wiring (Sources/RielaApp/NoteWindowController.swift:35) calls it without the override while passing allowEnvironmentOverrides: true for the other two. So a user who sets RIELA_NOTE_LINK_EXTRACT_WORKFLOW_DIR to point at a customized note-link-extract bundle gets it silently ignored: proposals run against the bundled examples copy (or the provider is nil if the bundle lacks the workflow) with no error, while the analogous env vars for rewrite/QA work. Inconsistently, RIELA_NOTE_LINK_EXTRACT_RIELA_EXECUTABLE is still honored because it is passed as configuredPath, which bypasses the allowEnvironmentOverrides gate in resolvedRielaExecutablePath (RielaNoteWorkflowProviderSupport.swift:98).
confirmed.fix_sketch: Align the default (or the NoteWindowController call) with the other two providers: allowEnvironmentOverrides: true for the link-extract provider; also gate configuredPath env-sourced values consistently.
confirmed.verify_note: Every element of the finding checks out in the code. (1) Sources/RielaNoteUI/RielaNoteWorkflowLinkProposalProvider.swift:80 — defaultProvider's allowEnvironmentOverrides parameter defaults to false, while the sibling providers' defaultProvider default it to true (RielaNoteWorkflowEditRewriteProvider.swift:99, RielaNoteWorkflowSelectionQuestionProvider.swift:99). (2) Sources/RielaApp/NoteWindowController.swift:35 calls RielaWorkflowNoteLinkProposalProvider.defaultProvider(environment:) without the override, while lines 36-43 explicitly pass allowEnvironmentOverrides: true for the edit-rewrite and selection-question providers. (3) defaultWorkflowDirectoryCandidates (RielaNoteWorkflowProviderSupport.swift:117-134) only appends the env-var directory when allowEnvironmentOverrides is true, so RIELA_NOTE_LINK_EXTRACT_WORKFLOW_DIR is silently dropped and only the bundle examples dir is considered; if the bundled note-link-extract/workflow.json is absent, defaultProvider returns nil silently (no error surfaced). (4) The claimed inconsistency is real: RIELA_NOTE_LINK_EXTRACT_RIELA_EXECUTABLE is passed as configuredPath (LinkProposalProvider.swift:88) and resolvedRielaExecutablePath (Support.swift:98-101) honors configuredPath unconditionally, bypassing the allowEnvironmentOverrides gate — so one env var works and the other is ignored. Additionally, git history shows this is a regression, not intentional lockdown: before commit 31f07fc the link provider honored RIELA_NOTE_LINK_EXTRACT_WORKFLOW_DIR unconditionally (old defaultWorkflowDirectoryCandidates at 31f07fc~1:Sources/RielaNoteUI/RielaNoteWorkflowLinkProposalProvider.swift:244); the gate was introduced when the shared helpers were refactored for the new providers, and only the two new call sites were updated to pass true. No test asserts the ignore behavior. Medium severity is appropriate: functionality degrades silently for env-configured users, no crash or data loss.

confirmed.title: Config-agent slug generation produces workflow ids the scaffolder rejects, leaving a half-applied proposal
confirmed.detail: noteConfigSlug keeps every Unicode alphanumeric (CharacterSet.alphanumerics includes CJK, Cyrillic, etc.), so a config-agent message in Japanese yields workflowId "note-ingest-<CJK chars>" which NoteIngestionWorkflowScaffolder.isSafeWorkflowId (Sources/RielaNote/NoteWorkflowScaffolder.swift:44) rejects (only a-z, 0-9, '-'). applyNoteConfigAgentProposal (line 613) is non-transactional: defineTagClass, defineTag, and configureAutoAction have already committed by the time scaffold() throws. Failure scenario: user types a non-ASCII config request and clicks apply; they get 'workflow id must contain lowercase letters, numbers, and dashes' implying nothing happened, but the tag class, tag, and an ENABLED auto-action routing note-created events were persisted, while the ingestion workflow bundle was never scaffolded.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Restrict noteConfigSlug to ASCII [a-z0-9] (transliterate or fall back to the default slug), and/or validate the workflowId before mutating the DB, applying scaffold first or rolling back tag/auto-action on scaffold failure.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. noteConfigSlug (Sources/RielaNoteUI/RielaNoteUIClient.swift:725) retains all Unicode alphanumerics (CharacterSet.alphanumerics = categories L*/M*/N*, including CJK/Cyrillic), so a non-ASCII config message produces workflowId "note-ingest-<non-ASCII>". NoteIngestionWorkflowScaffolder.isSafeWorkflowId (Sources/RielaNote/NoteWorkflowScaffolder.swift:83) only accepts a-z/0-9/'-' and scaffold() throws invalidInput at line 44. applyNoteConfigAgentProposal (RielaNoteUIClient.swift:613) is indeed non-transactional: defineTagClass, defineTag, and configureAutoAction each commit in their own SQLite transactions (NoteService+Catalog.swift, AutoActionDispatching.swift) with only non-empty/filter-JSON validation that CJK passes, before scaffold() throws; there is no rollback. The sole caller, RielaNoteConfigAgentViewModel.applyProposal, just sets state=.failed on error, leaving the persisted tag class, tag, and enabled note-created auto-action while the ingestion workflow bundle was never written. No guard anywhere prevents the scenario; tests only exercise ASCII messages.

confirmed.title: acceptLinkProposal failure poisons global LoadState, hiding the whole notebook list
confirmed.detail: The finding is correct at the cited location, with two nuances: (1) this is not unique to acceptLinkProposal — the entire RielaNoteLibraryViewModel uses the same set-global-state-to-.failed pattern for every mutation (linkSelectedNote:890, applyTagToSelectedNote:554, addCommentToSelectedNote:585, saveSelectedNoteBody:530, removeTagFromSelectedNote:568, etc.), so it is a pervasive design flaw and any fix should consider all mutation paths; (2) the ContentUnavailableView is an .overlay, so it obscures the notebook list rather than literally replacing it, but the user still sees a misleading full 'Unable to load notes' error screen that persists until some other state-mutating action.
confirmed.fix_sketch: On failure set linkProposalError (and restore state to .loaded) instead of state = .failed; avoid using the global LoadState for per-item link operations.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. acceptLinkProposal (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel+LinkProposals.swift:48-62) sets the global state to .loading and on error to .failed. NoteService.linkNotes (NoteService+Relations.swift:14-15) calls requireNote(toNoteId) which throws NoteServiceError.notFound when the target note is gone; the guard at line 43 only checks the in-memory proposal list, not target existence, so a stale AI proposal after external deletion (the store is watched via noteStoreChangeObservationURLs, RielaNoteRootView.swift:231) reaches the throw. RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:94-95 renders .failed as a ContentUnavailableView('Unable to load notes') overlay covering the sidebar, and nothing resets state until another load/mutation (e.g. Cmd+R). A local error channel (linkProposalError) already exists in the same file and is rendered locally in RielaNoteLinkSearchSheet.swift:272, so the global-state use here produces a misleading, sticky whole-list failure screen for a single failed link accept.

confirmed.title: Stale-result early returns leave isSelectionQuestionLoading/isEditRewriteLoading stuck true, permanently blocking the feature
confirmed.detail: The defect is real but not strictly permanent: selecting any note or notebook (selectNote/selectNotebook) calls advanceSelectionGeneration, which resets the stuck loading flags — the wedge persists only while the user stays on the newly created note (refresh() does NOT recover it). Also, askSelectionQuestion and proposeBodyRewrite are currently invoked only from tests (no view wires them up), so this is a latent view-model API defect rather than a reachable UI bug today; the finding itself acknowledged both points.
confirmed.fix_sketch: In every stale-return path reset the corresponding isLoading flag (only the published result must be dropped), or have createUserMemo/createNoteInSelectedNotebook call advanceSelectionGeneration like selectNote does.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. The stale guards in askSelectionQuestion (+SelectionQA.swift:40,53,61) and proposeBodyRewrite (+EditRewrite.swift:32,39) return without resetting isSelectionQuestionLoading/isEditRewriteLoading. createUserMemo (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:403) and createNoteInSelectedNotebook (line 435) replace selectedDetail with a different note without bumping the generations (advanceSelectionGeneration at lines 733-752 is the only reset path, called only by selectNote/selectNotebook), so the guard fails on noteId with an unchanged generation and the loading flag stays true. RielaNoteDetailView's onChange(of: noteId) resetEditingState (RielaNoteDetailView.swift:619) only clears translate + link-proposal state, and clearSelectionQAState/clearEditRewriteState/markSelectionQuestionSelectionStale have no view callers.
