Subsequent calls then short-circuit on the !isLoading guards, and in the post-addComment case (line 53) the agent comment is persisted while didSaveSelectionQuestionComment stays false and the detail is never refreshed.

confirmed.title: Untrusted note markdown is interpolated unescaped into a codex-agent prompt whose output translate auto-applies
confirmed.detail: Finding is correct with two minor caveats: (1) the preserved model-auth env vars are explicitly intentional per the in-code comment in RielaNoteWorkflowProviderSupport.swift (env-key auth is a supported path), so they are amplifying context for injection impact rather than a defect themselves; (2) no OCR-to-note-body ingestion path exists in Sources/RielaNote — the realistic untrusted-content vectors are S3-synced note stores (S3NoteFileStore.swift) and pasted/imported content. Additionally, the impact is slightly worse than stated: NoteService.updateNoteBody stores no revision history (destructive overwrite) and triggers noteUpdated auto-actions on the injected replacement body.
confirmed.category: injection of untrusted note content into workflows
confirmed.fix_sketch: Treat note content as data: pass it via a temp file or clearly delimited/escaped block with an explicit 'content is untrusted data, never instructions' system rule, and gate any auto-apply path on user confirmation.
confirmed.verify_note: Every load-bearing claim checks out in the code. noteEditRewriteArguments (RielaNoteWorkflowEditRewriteProvider.swift:214) passes raw bodyMarkdown/instruction/selectedText into workflowInput; renderPromptTemplate (Sources/RielaCore/PromptTemplate.swift:46-47) substitutes {{workflowInput.bodyMarkdown}} verbatim with no escaping, so a body containing a closing ``` fence breaks out of the prompt's markdown fence and injects agent-level instructions. translateSelectedNote (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel+Translation.swift:32) applies draft.rewrittenMarkdown via updateNoteBody immediately with no review, no empty-output guard, and NoteService.updateNoteBody keeps no revision history (no undo) and even fires noteUpdated auto-actions. The link-proposal provider (RielaNoteWorkflowLinkProposalProvider.swift:192, subjectBodyMarkdown) and selection-question provider share the pattern, and rielaWorkflowSanitizedEnvironment (RielaNoteWorkflowProviderSupport.swift:156) preserves model-auth keys into the spawned agent env as claimed. S3NoteFileStore.swift exists, so third-party-origin note content is realistic.

confirmed.title: All write-operation failures render as a full-screen 'Unable to load notes' overlay that hides the list
confirmed.detail: The single LoadState drives every operation (load, search, save body, add/remove tag, add comment, link note, create note). Any `.failed` state shows ContentUnavailableView('Unable to load notes') as an overlay covering the entire list (lines 93-95), blocking row taps until some later operation succeeds. Example: adding a duplicate-protected tag or a failed link ('protectedTag' error) replaces the whole notebook list with 'Unable to load notes' even though notes loaded fine, and the raw Swift error description (String(describing:)) is shown as the message. In compact layout the overlay is on a different screen from where the action happened, so the actual action appears to silently no-op.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Separate list-load errors from per-action errors: keep the overlay only for load/refresh/search failures and surface mutation failures inline (banner/toast near the control), with human-readable messages for NoteServiceError cases.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:93-95 renders ContentUnavailableView("Unable to load notes") as a full overlay whenever the shared viewModel.state is .failed. Every write operation in RielaNoteLibraryViewModel (saveSelectedNoteBody:530, applyTagToSelectedNote:554, removeTagFromSelectedNote:568, addCommentToSelectedNote:585, createUserMemo:417, createNoteInSelectedNotebook:450, linkSelectedNote:890) sets that same state to .failed(String(describing: error)), so raw error descriptions are shown and the notebook list is hidden even though notes loaded fine. NoteServiceError.protectedTag is a real thrown error (NoteService.swift:681/684, NoteService+NotebookTags.swift:43/46). The write ops are invoked from RielaNoteDetailView and RielaNoteLinkSearchSheet, which share the view model; RielaNoteDetailView has no error surfacing at all, and the link sheet calls onLinked() unconditionally even on failure. In compact layout (RielaNoteRootView.swift:96-137) the detail is a separate NavigationStack screen, so the failure overlay appears on the list screen away from where the action happened, making the action appear to silently no-op. The state only clears when a later operation succeeds.

confirmed.title: Externally deleted selected note makes every refresh fail into the error overlay
confirmed.detail: Core defect confirmed: an externally deleted selected note makes every refresh() fail into the error overlay because line 249 refetches the stale selectedNoteId and the catch never clears selectedDetail, so watcher-triggered and manual refreshes loop on the same notFound error. However, recovery is easier than claimed: ContentUnavailableView in a SwiftUI .overlay is not fully hit-blocking (only its centered label region intercepts taps), and the list itself was already successfully reloaded before the failing detail fetch, so tapping another note row (selectNote) or notebook (selectNotebook) replaces the selection and returns state to .loaded. No search or window restart is required, but the persistent error overlay and self-repeating refresh failure remain a real UX defect.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Catch notFound for the selected note/notebook during refresh and selectNote, clear the stale selection, and fall back to the first available note instead of failing the whole view.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code: refresh() (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:248-249) unconditionally fetches noteDetail for the remembered selection; noteDetail -> detail() -> service.getNote() throws NoteServiceError.notFound for a deleted note (RielaNoteUIClient.swift:646-647, NoteService.swift:469/944); the catch sets state=.failed without clearing selectedDetail, so the store-change watcher (RielaNoteRootView.swift:230-234, which fires on exactly this external write), scenePhase activation, toolbar Refresh, and pull-to-refresh all repeat the identical failure, and the .failed state shows the 'Unable to load notes' overlay over the list (RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:93-95). Only the recovery claim is overstated.

confirmed.title: Config-agent tab has no error display at all and loses the typed message on failure
confirmed.detail: RielaNoteConfigAgentViewModel.submitDraft clears draftMessage before awaiting and maps failures to state=.failed (RielaNoteConfigAgentViewModel.swift:34-41), and applyProposal does the same; but RielaNoteConfigAgentView renders only the workflow-root bar, proposal list, and composer — there is no view of `state`/error anywhere (contrast RielaNoteAgentView's errorBanner). Concrete scenario: user asks the config agent or presses Apply and the workflow scaffold fails (e.g., unwritable workflowRoot path typed in the bar); nothing visible happens — no proposal appears, no error, and the typed request text is gone (unlike the Agent tab, the draft is not restored).
confirmed.fix_sketch: Add an error banner bound to a config-agent errorMessage, restore draftMessage on submit failure, and mark failed Apply attempts on the proposal card.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. RielaNoteConfigAgentView.body (lines 10-32) renders only the workflow-root bar, proposal list, and composer; it never reads viewModel.state, so .failed(...) (set in RielaNoteConfigAgentViewModel.submitDraft lines 39-41 and applyProposal lines 56-58) is never displayed — no error banner and no loading indicator. submitDraft clears draftMessage (line 34) before awaiting and does not restore it in the catch block, so the typed message is silently lost on failure. The sibling Agent tab proves this is unintentional: RielaNoteAgentViewModel restores draftMessage on failure (lines 62-63) and RielaNoteAgentView shows errorBanner (lines 19, 67). The parent RielaNoteRootView (line 69) embeds the config view directly with no wrapper that could surface errors. The failure path is reachable: applyProposal passes the freely-editable workflowRoot text to client.applyNoteConfigAgentProposal, and any throw (e.g., unwritable path) results in no visible change.

confirmed.title: Turning off Temp chat mid-conversation strands earlier turns as permanently unsavable
confirmed.detail: The finding is correct except that the earlier turns are not strictly "permanently" unsavable: toggling Temp chat back ON re-enables the Save button, and saveTemporaryConversation (lines 76-77) would then append the unsaved turns 1-3 into the existing autoSaveNotebookId notebook (out of order, after turn 4). However, this recovery path is undiscoverable (re-enabling a toggle labeled "Temp chat (not saved)" to save), and in the natural flow the earlier turns are silently lost when the window closes, so the defect stands.
confirmed.fix_sketch: On the first non-temp save (saveOrAppend creating the notebook), persist all turns with empty persistedNoteIds, not just the new turn; or keep the Save button enabled whenever hasUnsavedTurns regardless of the toggle.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. isTemporaryChat is a plain @Published var bound directly to a Toggle (RielaNoteAgentView.swift:40) with no didSet/guard, so it can be flipped off mid-conversation. With temp ON, submitDraft (line 55) skips persistence, leaving turns with empty persistedNoteIds and autoSaveNotebookId nil. After toggling OFF, the next submitDraft calls saveOrAppend (lines 108-115), which persists only the single new turn and creates the notebook — it never sweeps up earlier unsaved turns. canSaveTemporaryConversation (lines 31-33) requires isTemporaryChat, so the Save button is disabled despite hasUnsavedTurns being true, and the toolbar shows the notebook id implying the conversation is saved. Turns are in-memory only, so earlier turns are silently lost on close, contradicting the auto-save-unless-temp contract in design-docs/riela-note-design.md:20.

confirmed.title: 'Translate note' overwrites and persists the note body in one click with no preview or undo
confirmed.detail: translateSelectedNote immediately calls `client.updateNoteBody(noteId:, bodyMarkdown: draft.rewrittenMarkdown)` (line 32) after the AI rewrite returns, destroying the original-language body. There is no confirmation, no draft-review step (README's stated contract for agent rewrites is 'nothing is persisted until you Save'), no undo, and the original markdown captured at line 16 is discarded. A mistranslation, wrong target language in the free-text field, or a garbled model response permanently replaces the note content; additionally the rewrite runs up to 120s and then writes over whatever the note contains at completion without any updatedAt/version check, so an external edit made meanwhile is clobbered.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Land the translation in the edit draft (bodyDraft) for review before Save, or at minimum confirm and auto-save the previous body as a comment/backup and verify updatedAt before writing.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. RielaNoteLibraryViewModel+Translation.swift:32 persists draft.rewrittenMarkdown via client.updateNoteBody immediately after the agent rewrite returns; the button in RielaNoteDetailView.swift:178 invokes it with no confirmation, and originalMarkdown (line 16) is never retained. NoteService.updateNoteBody is a plain SQL UPDATE with no revision history or updated_at/version precondition, so there is no undo. The rewrite provider deadline is 120s (RielaNoteWorkflowEditRewriteProvider.swift:34) and the post-await isCurrentTranslation guard only checks a generation counter and selected note ID, not body staleness — unlike the sibling edit-rewrite flow which explicitly guards against 'the draft changed while the edit agent was running' (EditRewrite.swift:58). External edits via GraphQL/CLI/auto-actions during the window are silently clobbered. Minor nuance only: the README:112 'nothing is persisted until you Save' contract is worded for the edit pill and the translate feature is undocumented, but translation uses the same agent-rewrite pathway, so the inconsistency and the destructive failure scenario both stand.

confirmed.title: Switching notes while editing silently discards the draft (pager shortcuts stay active in edit mode)
confirmed.detail: onChange(of: detail.note.noteId) calls resetEditingState() which wipes bodyDraft, draftTagName, and draftCommentMarkdown without confirmation (lines 54-57, 619-625). While the body editor is open, the note pager (cmd+left/cmd+right, lines 227-258) and the list rows remain fully enabled, so a single cmd+arrow keystroke — easy to hit while editing text — navigates away and irreversibly deletes the in-progress edit and any half-typed comment. No warning, no way back.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Disable the pager/navigation while bodyDraft.isEditingBody (or prompt to keep/discard changes), and preserve per-note drafts keyed by noteId instead of resetting on selection change.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. RielaNoteDetailView.swift lines 54-57: onChange(of: detail.note.noteId) calls resetEditingState(), which (lines 619-625) calls bodyDraft.reset() (lines 709-713, wiping draftBodyMarkdown/editingNoteId/isEditingBody) and clears draftTagName and draftCommentMarkdown with no confirmation and no recovery path. The notePager (lines 226-258) is rendered unconditionally, binds cmd+left/cmd+right keyboard shortcuts, and is disabled only by index-bounds properties canSelectPreviousNote/canSelectNextNote (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:146-158) — no isEditingBody guard, even though the sibling translationControl in the same view does disable itself on bodyDraft.isEditingBody (lines 176, 195). selectAdjacentNote (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:649-661) has no editing guard and cannot have one since bodyDraft is view-local @State. On macOS, SwiftUI command-key equivalents take precedence over the focused text view's cmd+arrow line-start/line-end navigation, so hitting cmd+left while editing (a standard text-navigation habit) navigates away and destroys the draft; clicking another list row does the same. The failure scenario is real as described.

confirmed.title: Comment and tag drafts are cleared even when the add operation fails
confirmed.detail: The Add comment button does `await viewModel.addCommentToSelectedNote(bodyMarkdown); draftCommentMarkdown = ""` (lines 525-530) and Add tag does the same with draftTagName (583-587); both clear the field regardless of outcome because addCommentToSelectedNote/applyTagToSelectedNote swallow errors into state=.failed. Scenario: user writes a multi-paragraph comment, the insert fails (locked DB / note deleted externally) — the comment text disappears from the editor, no comment appears, and the only signal is the 'Unable to load notes' overlay on the other column/screen.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Return success from the view-model mutations and clear the drafts only on success; show inline failure text under the comment editor / tag field.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. RielaNoteDetailView.swift lines 525-530 and 582-587 clear draftCommentMarkdown/draftTagName unconditionally after awaiting the view model calls. RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.addCommentToSelectedNote (lines 572-587) and applyTagToSelectedNote (lines 534-556) return Void and swallow all thrown errors into state = .failed, so the caller cannot detect failure. The .failed state is only surfaced as the 'Unable to load notes' ContentUnavailableView in RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:95 (the list column), not near the editors. A failed insert therefore silently destroys the user's typed comment/tag text exactly as described.

confirmed.title: UI-created memos never receive the seeded 'notebook-kind:user-memo' notebook kind tag
confirmed.detail: The defect is real but anchored to createUserMemo (RielaNoteUIClient.swift:338-347), which mints a new "User Memo" notebook via NoteService.createNote without any notebook-kind tag. The finding's inclusion of createNote(inNotebook:) (lines 349-358) is slightly overbroad: that method only adds a note to an already-existing notebook and does not create an untagged notebook itself. All downstream consequences (missing kind chip, tagFilter ["notebook-kind:user-memo"] misses, auto-action notebookKindTag filters not matching, contrast with agent-conversation notebooks that do get their kind tag) are accurate.
confirmed.fix_sketch: In createUserMemo (and the implicit-notebook branch of NoteService.createNote), apply notebook-kind:user-memo as a system, non-deletable notebook tag when a new notebook is auto-created for a user memo.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. createUserMemo (RielaNoteUIClient.swift:338-347) routes through NoteService.createNote (NoteService.swift:84-182), which has no kindTagName parameter and inserts the new notebook row with no notebook_tags entry; only the note-level topic tag "user-memo" is applied. The design doc (design-docs/specs/design-riela-note.md:456) requires quick memos to be notebooks tagged notebook-kind:user-memo, the schema seeds that protected tag (NoteStoreSchema.swift:295), the notebook list kind chip is derived from notebook-kind:* notebook tags (RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:294-299), and the agent-conversation path does apply its kind tag (NoteService+Relations.swift:268-298), proving the asymmetry. No caller or view-model path compensates (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:392/427). So UI-created memo notebooks show no kind chip and are invisible to notebook-kind:user-memo tag filters used by CLI/GraphQL/auto-action filters.

confirmed.title: Non-image file attachments can be 'resolved' but never opened, viewed, or saved
confirmed.detail: Tapping an attachment button calls viewModel.selectFileAttachment; for non-image files it only sets selectedResolvedFile (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:499-510), and the sole UI result is a one-line caption with filename/byte-size/storage-kind (lines 422-432). The design's core PDF/YouTube use cases attach source PDFs, videos, and audio ('関連fileはスムースに参照でき'), and the whole file's Data is downloaded (including from S3), but there is no player, QuickLook, open-in-Finder, or export affordance — the bytes are fetched and dropped. Users cannot access any attached video/audio/PDF content from the app.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Add an open/share path for resolved files: write to a temp file and QuickLook/NSWorkspace.open on macOS, or offer a fileExporter like the markdown download.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. RielaNoteDetailView.swift:404-417 wires attachment buttons to viewModel.selectFileAttachment; for non-image files RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:499-510 only sets selectedResolvedFile, whose sole UI consumption is the caption at RielaNoteDetailView.swift:422-432 (filename · byte size + storageKind rawValue). RielaNoteUIClient.resolveFile (lines 418-431) downloads the file's entire Data, including from S3, but for non-image attachments the .data field is never consumed by any view — the only view rendering RielaNoteResolvedFile is the image-only RielaNoteSourceImageView. Grep across Sources/RielaNoteUI finds no QuickLook, NSWorkspace, NSSavePanel, ShareLink, or fileExporter for attachment bytes (the only fileExporter is the note-body markdown export). design-docs/riela-note-design.md lines 13-14 confirm the PDF ("関連fileはスムースに参照でき") and YouTube-video-attachment use cases, so attached PDF/video/audio content is genuinely inaccessible from the app while still being fully downloaded on tap.

confirmed.title: Symbol-only search queries silently return no results because the LIKE fallback only runs in the FTS branch
confirmed.detail: ftsTerms() splits on non-alphanumeric scalars, so a query containing no alphanumeric characters (e.g. "→", ":::", "😀", "<->") yields ftsMatchQuery == nil and control goes to searchNotesByFilters, which returns [] when no tag/class/date filters are set (line 158) - the searchNotesByTextLike LIKE fallback (which would find literal substring matches) is never invoked in this branch, only in the FTS branch (line 115). Concrete failure: a note body contains "steps: A → B"; user searches "→" in the note search UI -> zero results returned even though a plain LIKE scan matches; with filters set, the query text is ignored entirely and all filter matches are returned as if the query matched.
confirmed.fix_sketch: In the nil-matchQuery branch, when the trimmed query is non-empty, run searchNotesByTextLike (with the same filters) instead of/merged with searchNotesByFilters.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading Sources/RielaNote/NoteSearch.swift. ftsTerms (line 642) splits on non-alphanumeric scalars, so a symbol-only query ("→", ":::", "😀") produces no terms and ftsMatchQuery returns nil; the guard at line 29 then routes to searchNotesByFilters, which returns [] with no tag/class/date filters (line 158) and ignores the query text entirely when filters are set (snippet even built with query: "" at line 219). The searchNotesByTextLike LIKE fallback — which would match such queries via %→% against title/body/tags — is only invoked in the FTS branch (line 115) and is unreachable in the nil-match branch. The public NoteService.searchNotes passes user queries through unguarded, so the failure is reachable. The empty-query filter-listing behavior is intentional, but the non-empty symbol-only case bypassing an explicitly-built zero-result LIKE fallback is an oversight (calling the fallback there would still no-op for empty queries due to its own trimmed-empty guard at line 238). No tests cover symbol-only queries.

confirmed.title: createNotebookWithNotes: implicit (index+1) and explicit page numbers can collide, failing ingest with an opaque UNIQUE-constraint error
confirmed.detail: validateNotebookIngestPageNumbers only checks that EXPLICIT noteNumbers are positive and mutually unique. insertNotebookWithNotes assigns `page.noteNumber ?? index + 1` (NoteService.swift:262), so mixed input like pages = [NotePageDraft(body1), NotePageDraft(body2, noteNumber: 1)] assigns note_number 1 to both (implicit index 0 -> 1, explicit -> 1) and the INSERT violates UNIQUE(notebook_id, note_number). Concrete failure: the whole ingest transaction rolls back (no corruption) but the caller receives a raw SQLite constraint error instead of the NoteServiceError.invalidInput the validator is supposed to produce, making a validatable input look like a database failure.
confirmed.fix_sketch: During validation (or assignment), compute the effective number for every page (explicit or positional) and reject/skip collisions deterministically, e.g. assign implicit pages the next unused number.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. validateNotebookIngestPageNumbers (Sources/RielaNote/NoteService+Rows.swift:144-157) explicitly skips nil noteNumbers and only dedupes explicit ones; insertNotebookWithNotes (Sources/RielaNote/NoteService.swift:262) assigns `page.noteNumber ?? index + 1`, so an implicit index+1 can equal another page's explicit number; the notes table has UNIQUE(notebook_id, note_number) (Sources/RielaNote/NoteStoreSchema.swift:326), so the INSERT fails with a raw SQLite constraint error instead of NoteServiceError.invalidInput. The path is reachable: the riela/notebook-ingest-pages addon (Sources/RielaCLI/ProductionNodeAdapter+NoteAddons.swift:544) parses per-page number/pageNumber as optional, so mixed implicit/explicit user JSON flows straight into createNotebookWithNotes with no guard against mixing. The transaction wrapper means a clean rollback with no corruption, exactly as the finding states — the defect is the opaque error surface for a validatable input.

confirmed.title: dispatchAutoActions(for:) swallows enqueue errors, silently dropping auto-action events
confirmed.detail: AutoActionDispatching.swift:149: `try?` in dispatchAutoActions(for:) genuinely swallows enqueue-transaction errors with no persisted row, no diagnostic, and no retry — but this method is currently dead code within the repo (no callers in Sources or Tests); it is only exposed as public API of the RielaNote library, so the loss can only bite external/future callers. All in-repo auto-action triggers (NoteService.swift:68/158/167/298/307/575, NoteService+Relations.swift:141/191/200) use `try enqueueAutoActions` inside the parent write transaction and propagate errors, so the "new note AI-tagging silently lost" scenario does not occur via existing flows. NoteService.swift:27-28 `try?` does hide startup recovery failures, but affected rows remain persisted (pending/in-flight) and are re-attempted on a later successful init or explicit retryPendingAutoActionDispatches call — degraded/delayed, not permanent loss. Net: a real but latent low-severity trap (public API silent-loss + missing diagnostics), not an active data-loss bug.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Propagate the error (the method is only called from non-throwing contexts by choice) or at minimum route it through autoActionDiagnosticRecorder so operators can see dropped events.
confirmed.verify_note: The cited code is real and behaves as described: dispatchAutoActions(for:) wraps the enqueue transaction in try?, so any error leaves no persisted dispatch row, no diagnostic, and no retry path — silent permanent loss for that event. It is public API of the exported RielaNote library. However, the finding overstates reach: the method has zero callers in Sources or Tests, and every actual production trigger path (createNotebook, note create/update, tag/relation ops) calls `try enqueueAutoActions` inside the parent transaction and propagates errors, so the cited example (AI tagging for a new note silently lost) cannot happen through real flows today. The NoteService.init try? part is real but only delays recovery (rows persist and are retried on next init), not permanent loss.

confirmed.title: Uploaded S3 object is not compensated when the DB update or verifyRemoteRead fails mid-migration
confirmed.detail: In migrateFileStorage the S3 PUT (line 36) succeeds before the DB transaction (lines 56-83) and the optional verifyRemoteRead GET (line 53). If the DB update throws (e.g., SQLITE_BUSY from a concurrent writer) or the verify read fails, the function throws with the record still storage_kind='local', but the already-uploaded object at <prefix>/<fileId> stays in the bucket with no compensating s3Store.delete. A later retry re-uploads to the same key so state self-heals only if the operator retries; if the failure is permanent or the migration is abandoned, the bucket carries orphaned billable objects with nothing pointing at them.
confirmed.category: migration edge case / S3 error handling
confirmed.fix_sketch: Wrap the DB update + verify in do/catch and issue a best-effort s3Store.delete(record: pendingRecord) on failure before rethrowing.
confirmed.verify_note: Verified in Sources/RielaNote/NoteFileMigration.swift: the S3 PUT (line 36) precedes the optional verifyRemoteRead GET (line 53) and the DB transaction (lines 56-83), and the function has no catch/defer/compensating s3Store.delete on any failure path. S3NoteFileStore.delete exists (S3NoteFileStore.swift:77) but is never invoked. The caller migrateAllLocalFiles just records the error as a NoteFileMigrationFailure without cleanup. storageKey is deterministic (<prefix>/<fileId>, S3NoteFileStore.swift:90-96), so a retry overwrites the same key and self-heals, but an abandoned or permanently failing migration leaves an orphaned billable object with the DB row still storage_kind='local'. The finding's scenario is real and its low severity is appropriate.

confirmed.title: Crash window between blob write and DB insert leaves unreferenced blobs with no reconciliation sweep
confirmed.detail: attachFile/attachNotebookFile write the blob first (`fileStore.store` at lines 19/73/129/182/229) and insert the files row afterwards inside a separate withDatabase transaction. If the process is killed between the two steps (ctrl-C on a CLI attach, crash, OOM), the blob exists under files/<shard>/<fileId> (or in S3) with no DB row, and since fileIds are unique per attempt the retry writes a new fileId — the stray blob is never referenced or cleaned by any code path. Similarly, a kill during LocalNoteFileStore.store between the temp write and the rename leaves `.file-*.tmp-*` files that no sweeper removes (the defer at LocalNoteFileStore.swift:21 only runs on normal error paths). Impact is limited to leaked disk/S3 space accumulating across crashes.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Add a startup/maintenance sweep that removes files/<shard> entries and .tmp files with no matching files row older than some grace period (could be part of the same GC pass as the files-table reclamation).
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. All five attach variants in NoteService+Files.swift call fileStore.store (lines 19/73/129/182/229) before the DB transaction that inserts the files row; the only blob cleanup is the in-process catch block, which cannot run if the process is killed between the two steps. makeNoteId produces a unique timestamp+UUID fileId per attempt, so retries never reuse the orphan's path, and no sweep/reconciliation/GC code exists anywhere in Sources that scans files/<shard> or S3 against the DB. The tmp-file claim also holds: LocalNoteFileStore writes .<fileId>.tmp-<uuid> then renames, and the defer cleanup only runs on normal unwinding (return/throw), not process death; nothing ever cleans stray .tmp- files. Impact is correctly characterized as leaked disk/S3 space only.

confirmed.title: SQLiteNoteDatabaseDriver exposes mutable databasePath/openOptions that silently diverge from the actual connection
confirmed.detail: The divergence mechanism is real: SQLiteNoteDatabaseDriver exposes databasePath/openOptions as public vars though the NoteDatabaseDriving protocol only requires get, and the private connection captures init-time values and caches the open database, so mutating the struct after init silently leaves withDatabase targeting the original file. Callers do derive file operations from driver.databasePath (RielaNoteUIClient.swift:361-372 DB/WAL/SHM URLs; NoteService+Files.swift:350). But no code in the repository mutates these properties post-init — every construction site sets the path at init only — so this is a latent public-API footgun (fields should be let), not a currently manifesting bug. Low severity is appropriate.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Make databasePath and openOptions 'let' (public private(set) at minimum) so the advertised path can never diverge from the connection actually in use.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code: databasePath/openOptions are public vars (lines 11-12) while the private SQLiteNoteDatabaseConnection captures init-time values (lines 21, 27, 47-50) and caches the opened SQLiteDatabase (line 57-58), never re-reading the struct properties. Post-init mutation compiles and silently diverges exactly as described, and databasePath is used for real file decisions (RielaNoteUIClient.swift:361-372 backup/WAL paths, NoteService+Files.swift:350). However, a full grep of Sources/ and Tests/ shows no caller ever mutates these properties after init, so the failure is latent rather than currently triggered.

confirmed.title: @skip/@include directives are silently ignored on nested selections and break parsing on root selections
confirmed.detail: skipGraphQLSelectionSuffix consumes directives on nested fields without evaluating them, so `bodyMarkdown @include(if: false)` still returns the field, and `@skip(if: true)` is not honored — spec-compliant clients relying on conditional selection get wrong response shapes (fields present that they asked to exclude). At the root level directives are not consumed at all: `{ tags @include(if: true) { result { accepted } } }` fails with the misleading error 'GraphQL root selection is missing a field name'. Nested field arguments are likewise silently discarded.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Either evaluate @skip/@include (values are simple booleans/variables) or reject documents containing directives with an explicit 'directives not supported' error at both root and nested levels.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading Sources/RielaGraphQL/NoteGraphQLDocumentParsing.swift and NoteGraphQLDocumentExecutor.swift. Nested selections: skipGraphQLSelectionSuffix (lines 318-341) consumes @directives and their argument lists without evaluation, and the field is unconditionally appended (line 309); the executor projects the response shape from these selections (projectGraphQLValue, lines 499-517), so @include(if:false)/@skip(if:true) fields are still returned. Root selections: parseGraphQLRootSelections never consumes directives — after a root field name, '@' is neither ':' nor '(' nor '{', so the next loop iteration's readGraphQLIdentifier fails on '@' and throws the exact misleading error 'GraphQL root selection is missing a field name' (line 225). The parse error is caught and returned as handled:true (executor lines 84-93), so there is no fallback to a directive-aware engine. Nested field arguments are likewise skipped via skipGraphQLBalanced (lines 323-325) and discarded. No tests cover @include/@skip, and fragments get explicit 'not supported' errors while directives are silently swallowed, so this is not coherent intentional design.

confirmed.title: optionalString coerces empty strings to nil, turning notes(notebookId: "") into an unscoped listing
confirmed.detail: optionalString returns nil for empty strings. For notes(notebookId:), an empty notebookId (e.g. a UI passing an unset form value) is treated as 'no notebook filter' and returns every note across all notebooks instead of a not_found/invalid_request error — the service-side requireNotebook check that would reject a bad id is silently bypassed. The same coercion silently swaps provenance "" for the "human" default in removeNoteTag/removeNotebookTag and drops empty createdAfter/createdBefore bounds instead of rejecting them.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Throw invalidVariable for present-but-empty string arguments (or at minimum for notebookId), instead of mapping them to nil.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by code reading. optionalString (NoteGraphQLDocumentExecutor.swift:752) returns nil for empty strings via `string.isEmpty ? nil : string`. For the notes query (line 137) this nil flows to NoteService.listNotes (NoteService.swift:503-507) where the `if let notebookId` guard skips both requireNotebook validation and the notebook_id predicate, so notes(notebookId: "") returns all notes across notebooks instead of a not_found error that any other invalid id would produce. The provenance claim also holds: lines 206/225 coerce "" to the "human" default, which bypasses the provenance parse failure a non-empty invalid string would hit and grants the privileged default (human provenance may remove human-assigned tags where ai may not, NoteService.swift:683). createdAfter/createdBefore "" bounds are likewise silently dropped (lines 130-131, 146-147). No caller guard or test establishes this as intentional; requiredString in the same file explicitly rejects "" as invalid, contradicting an intentional-design reading.

confirmed.title: Wrong-typed limit/offset arguments are silently ignored and replaced by defaults
confirmed.detail: optionalInt returns nil (rather than throwing invalidVariable) when the value is present but not numeric, e.g. `notes(limit: "5")` or variables {"limit": "abc"}: the string is discarded and the default limit 50 is applied with no error. This contrasts with optionalBool/optionalString/optionalStringArray which all throw on type mismatches, so clients sending Int arguments as JSON strings get 50 rows and no signal that their limit was dropped, which corrupts pagination loops that assume the requested page size.
confirmed.fix_sketch: In optionalInt, throw NoteGraphQLDocumentExecutorError.invalidVariable("\(key) must be an integer") when the value exists but is neither .integer nor an integral .number, mirroring optionalBool.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed. optionalInt (NoteGraphQLDocumentExecutor.swift:772-783) returns nil for any non-integer/non-number JSONValue (guard at line 776), so a string-typed limit/offset — either inline (`notes(limit: "5")`, parsed as .string by parseGraphQLValue line 396 with no schema coercion) or via variables (`{"limit": "abc"}`, passed through raw at NoteGraphQLDocumentParsing.swift:391-394) — is silently replaced by the default (boundedLimit default 50) with no error. This is asymmetric with optionalBool/optionalString/optionalStringArray, which all throw invalidVariable on type mismatch (lines 767, 750, 798/802). The behavior is not intentional: the regression test at Tests/RielaGraphQLTests/NoteGraphQLDocumentParsingRegressionTests.swift:105-115 asserts fractional limits (2.9) must error with "limit must be an integer", showing malformed limits are meant to be rejected, yet string limits pass silently. Low severity is fair since the query still succeeds with clamped defaults.

confirmed.title: Persistence failure after a successful agent answer silently discards the answered turn
confirmed.detail: In submitDraft, if answerNoteAgentTurn succeeds but saveOrAppend throws (e.g. DB busy/read-only), the whole turn is dropped: the generated answer is never appended to `turns`, so the user never sees it, and the restored draft only survives if the user hasn't typed anything new (line 62-64 restores the message only when draftMessage is empty — otherwise the failed message is lost entirely). The expensive/informative agent answer is thrown away even though it was produced.
confirmed.fix_sketch: On saveOrAppend failure, still append the turn with empty persistedNoteIds (it then shows and remains saveable via saveTemporaryConversation), and keep the failed message recoverable.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading Sources/RielaNoteUI/RielaNoteAgentViewModel.swift:46-67. If answerNoteAgentTurn (line 54) succeeds but saveOrAppend (line 56) throws, execution jumps to the catch block, skipping turns.append(turn) at line 59 — the generated answer is a local variable and is discarded, never shown to the user. The draft restore at lines 62-64 only happens if draftMessage is empty; the TextField (RielaNoteAgentView.swift:84) is not disabled while state == .loading, so text typed during the await prevents restoration of the failed message. No guard prevents saveOrAppend from throwing (both client persistence methods are throwing), and the test suite only covers answerNoteAgentTurn failure, not save-after-answer failure. The class already has unsaved-turn machinery (persistedNoteIds empty, appendUnsavedTurns) that could preserve the turn, so this is a genuine low-severity defect, not intentional-and-harmless behavior.

confirmed.title: applyProposal can run twice concurrently, duplicating config side effects
confirmed.detail: applyProposal (Sources/RielaNoteUI/RielaNoteConfigAgentViewModel.swift:44) can run twice concurrently for the same proposal: the Apply button in RielaNoteConfigProposalView is only disabled once appliedResult != nil, which is set after the apply completes, and state == .loading does not disable it. A double-click therefore executes applyNoteConfigAgentProposal twice. With the current NoteServiceRielaNoteUIClient this is benign — defineTagClass, defineTag, and configureAutoAction are ON CONFLICT upserts, the workflow scaffolder tolerates existing directories and overwrites files, and DB access is NSLock-serialized — so the second run succeeds with identical results rather than throwing. The defect is limited to duplicated idempotent work and a fragile design that depends on every RielaNoteUIClient implementation being idempotent; adding a loading/reentrancy guard (e.g., disable Apply while state == .loading or track an in-flight proposal id) would be a low-severity robustness fix.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Guard with `state != .loading` (and per-proposal in-flight tracking) and disable the Apply button while state is loading.
confirmed.verify_note: The reentrancy is real: applyProposal has no in-flight guard and the Apply button is disabled only once proposal.appliedResult != nil (set after completion), while the await on the nonisolated client is a genuine MainActor suspension point — so a double-click does issue applyNoteConfigAgentProposal twice concurrently. However, the finding's concrete failure consequence is wrong: every step is idempotent (defineTagClass/defineTag/configureAutoAction are SQL ON CONFLICT upserts; the workflow scaffolder uses createDirectory(withIntermediateDirectories:true) and overwriting atomic file writes, so re-scaffolding an existing directory does NOT throw), and all DB access is serialized by an NSLock in SQLiteNoteDatabaseConnection.withDatabase. The "second run throws and UI reports failure for a change that actually succeeded" scenario cannot happen with the shipped client.

confirmed.title: Quick-create plus button is mislabeled for accessibility
confirmed.detail: The primary floating button invokes onCreateMemo but has `.accessibilityLabel("New note")` while its help text says 'New memo' (lines 27-37). A VoiceOver user hears 'New note' and expects a note in the selected notebook (the distinct action offered elsewhere as 'New note'), but activating it creates a memo instead — inconsistent and misleading for assistive-tech users.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Change the accessibilityLabel to "New memo" to match the action and the help text.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading Sources/RielaNoteUI/RielaNoteQuickCreateButton.swift: the plus button (lines 27-37) calls onCreateMemo() but has .accessibilityLabel("New note") while its .help tooltip says "New memo". Within the same view, "New note" labels the distinct onCreateInNotebook action (line 22 and context menu line 41 "New note in notebook"), and the caller RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:87-88 maps onCreateMemo to .memo and onCreateInNotebook to .selectedNotebook. A VoiceOver user therefore hears the name of the notebook-scoped action on a button that creates a memo — a real accessibility mislabel with no mitigating guard.

confirmed.title: dispatchAutoActions swallows enqueue errors, silently losing auto-action events
confirmed.detail: The code claim is accurate: dispatchAutoActions(for:) (Sources/RielaNote/AutoActionDispatching.swift:149-153) wraps the enqueue transaction in `try? ... ?? []`, so an enqueue failure (e.g. SQLITE_BUSY) leaves no dispatch row and no diagnostic (autoActionDiagnosticRecorder is only used for filter-evaluation failures), and retryPendingAutoActionDispatches cannot recover never-enqueued events. However, the stated failure scenario overstates reach: dispatchAutoActions(for:) has zero callers anywhere in Sources or Tests — all real note/notebook events (createNote, createNotebook, updates, relations at NoteService.swift:68,158,167,298,307,575 and NoteService+Relations.swift:141,191,200) enqueue inside the note transaction with plain `try`, so internal events are not silently lost today. The defect is a latent hazard: the method is public API (inside `public extension NoteService`) on the exported RielaNote library product, so any external consumer calling this entry point would silently lose events exactly as described. The branch's own review doc (design-docs/specs/design-riela-note-branch-review.md, WP-1) previously flagged this same swallow and recommended making it throw or record diagnostics; that item remains unaddressed while other WP-1 items were implemented.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Log/record the enqueue error via autoActionDiagnosticRecorder (or propagate to the caller) instead of try?; consider a busy-retry.
confirmed.verify_note: The cited code confirms the swallow: try? coalesces enqueue failures to [] with no dispatch row, no diagnostic, and no retry path. But the concrete failure scenario (a note event's auto-actions silently lost) cannot occur via internal paths because dispatchAutoActions(for:) has no callers in the repo — all real event paths enqueue in-transaction with propagating errors. It is still a genuine latent defect on a public library API, previously flagged in the branch's own review doc and left unfixed, so the finding is partially right rather than wrong.

confirmed.title: Cancellation race: task cancellation before processBox.set leaves an orphan workflow running; in-loop Task.isCancelled checks are dead code
confirmed.detail: The work runs in Task.detached, which does not inherit the caller's cancellation, so the `if Task.isCancelled` checks inside runNoteEditRewriteWorkflow (line 180; same in the link and selection providers) can never fire. Cancellation is only effective via onCancel -> processBox.terminate(), which is a no-op if cancellation lands before process.run()/processBox.set (e.g., link provider first does a synchronous SQLite getNote at RielaNoteWorkflowLinkProposalProvider.swift:128). Failure scenario: the caller cancels immediately after invoking; the riela workflow process still spawns and runs an agent to the full 120 s deadline, consuming API tokens, with the result discarded; when terminate does land mid-run, the caller receives workflowFailed("") instead of CancellationError, surfacing a bogus error message for a user-initiated cancel.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Check a shared cancelled flag (set in onCancel) inside the poll loop and before process.run(); map signal-terminated exits after cancellation to CancellationError.
confirmed.verify_note: All three claims verified by reading the code. (1) The work runs in Task.detached whose handle is never cancelled and which inherits no cancellation from the caller, so the `if Task.isCancelled` checks at RielaNoteWorkflowEditRewriteProvider.swift:180 (and the analogous checks in the link/selection providers) are unreachable dead code. (2) RielaWorkflowProcessBox.terminate() (RielaNoteWorkflowProviderSupport.swift:24-29) is a no-op when the boxed process is nil and set() has no cancelled-latch, so a cancellation landing before processBox.set (line 172) leaves the spawned riela workflow process running to completion or the 120s deadline with no way to stop it; the link provider's synchronous SQLite getNote at RielaNoteWorkflowLinkProposalProvider.swift:128 widens that window, and the cancelled caller additionally blocks in `await .value` since the detached task is not cancellation-responsive. (3) When onCancel does terminate a running process, the loop exits on !isRunning, terminationStatus is nonzero (uncaught signal), and line 195-196 throws workflowFailed(trimmed stderr, typically "") to the caller instead of CancellationError. No caller guard or test prevents any of this, and the dead in-loop checks show cancellation propagation was intended, so it is a real defect rather than intentional behavior.

confirmed.title: Entire note body passed on the command line breaks for very large notes (ARG_MAX)
confirmed.detail: The full bodyMarkdown (plus selection) is serialized into the --variables argv string. Darwin limits argv+envp to ~1 MB (ARG_MAX), so for a note around/above that size (large pasted logs, big OCR-ingested bodies) process.run() fails with E2BIG and the rewrite/translate/selection features fail with an opaque low-level error; translate surfaces it as a raw error string. Same pattern in the selection-question and link-extract providers.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Write variables JSON to a temp file and pass a --variables-file (or stdin) to riela workflow run when payload exceeds a safe threshold.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. noteEditRewriteArguments (RielaNoteWorkflowEditRewriteProvider.swift:204-245) serializes the entire bodyMarkdown (plus selection) into the --variables argv string, and runNoteEditRewriteWorkflow launches the riela CLI via Process.run() (posix_spawn on Darwin, subject to ARG_MAX ~1 MB for argv+envp, with the full sanitized environment also attached at line 163). No size guard or truncation exists anywhere in the chain: translateSelectedNote (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel+Translation.swift:16-28) passes detail.note.bodyMarkdown unbounded, and RielaNoteUIClient.proposeNoteBodyRewrite forwards it untouched. The only truncation helper (limit 400) applies to a UI blockquote preview, not workflow input. A note body near/above ~1 MB (inflated further by JSON escaping) makes process.run() throw E2BIG, surfaced on the translate path as a raw error string via translateNoteErrorMessage. The same argv pattern is confirmed in RielaNoteWorkflowSelectionQuestionProvider.swift (lines 216, 234-235) and RielaNoteWorkflowLinkProposalProvider.swift (lines 137, 205-206, full subjectBodyMarkdown). Low severity is appropriate since it needs a very large note, but the failure scenario is real.

confirmed.title: Toggling 'Include linked notes' with no query or filters replaces the whole list with empty search results
confirmed.detail: hasSearchFilters counts filter.includeLinked (lines 120-125), so switching the Filters pane's 'Include linked notes' toggle on by itself flips isSearching to true and runs performSearch with an empty query. Server-side, ftsMatchQuery returns nil and searchNotesByFilters returns [] when tag/class/date filters are all empty (NoteSearch.swift:158), and linked-neighbor expansion requires non-empty direct results — so the result is always empty. The notebook list disappears and the user sees the 'No Results' empty state until they figure out that the toggle alone caused it.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Exclude includeLinked from hasSearchFilters (treat it as a search modifier, not a standalone filter), or disable the toggle until a query/tag/class/date filter is active.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the full chain. The Filters pane toggle (RielaNoteFilterPane.swift:94-99) calls updateIncludeLinked (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:904-906), which sets filter.includeLinked and runs performSearch. hasSearchFilters (lines 120-125) counts filter.includeLinked, so isSearching flips true and the performSearch guard at line 303 passes with an empty query. Server-side (Sources/RielaNote/NoteSearch.swift): empty query makes ftsMatchQuery return nil (line 627), searchNotesByFilters returns [] when tag/class/date filters are all absent (guard at line 158), and appendLinkedNeighborResults returns unchanged when directResults is empty (guard at line 336) — so the result is deterministically empty. RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:22-27 then replaces the notebooks list with the empty search results and the overlay at line 98 shows ContentUnavailableView.search ("No Results"). No guard anywhere prevents this; the finding is accurate including its low/ux-product severity.

confirmed.title: No loading indicator; 'No notes' empty state shows during initial load
confirmed.detail: The 'No notes' empty state at RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:96-97 does show during the initial load(): notebooks starts empty, state is .loading, and no ProgressView or loading branch exists in the library list view or its parent (RielaNoteRootView renders the list immediately and loads in .task). On a slow store users see 'No notes' for a non-empty library until the first listNotebooks call returns. But the claim that this also happens on "every refresh that momentarily has no data" is incorrect: refresh()/loadNotebooksFirstPage() only reassign notebooks after the fetch completes, so an already-populated list never momentarily empties during refresh. The defect is real but limited to the initial-load window (low severity UX issue, as filed).
confirmed.fix_sketch: Add `else if viewModel.state == .loading { ProgressView() }` before the empty-state branch and gate 'No notes' on state == .loaded.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code: the overlay at Sources/RielaNoteUI/RielaNoteNotebookListView.swift:96-97 shows ContentUnavailableView("No notes") whenever notebooks is empty and not searching, with no check of state == .loading and no ProgressView in the library list UI. RielaNoteRootView renders the list immediately and runs viewModel.load() in .task; load() sets state = .loading and awaits client.listNotebooks before populating notebooks (RielaNoteLibraryViewModel.swift:187-203, 663-668), so on a slow store the user sees "No notes" during the entire initial load with no loading signal. However, the "every refresh" claim is wrong: refresh() never clears notebooks before fetching (they are replaced only after the await completes), so a populated list does not flash "No notes" on refresh — the defect is limited to initial load.

confirmed.title: Deleting notes/notebooks orphans file rows and on-disk blobs
confirmed.detail: deleteNoteRows removes note_files join rows (line 903) but never deletes the corresponding `files` records or the stored blobs under <noteRoot>/files/ (LocalNoteFileStore) or S3 objects; deleteNotebook likewise only clears notebook_files. Scenario: user ingests a PDF notebook (one source-page image per page plus the source PDF), then deletes the notebook via `riela note delete`/deleteNotebook — potentially hundreds of MB of image blobs and files rows remain forever with no reference and no cleanup command.
confirmed.fix_sketch: After removing the join rows, delete files rows that no longer have any note_files/notebook_files references and remove local blobs (best-effort) — or provide a `riela note storage gc` command.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading the code. deleteNoteRows (NoteService.swift:891-910) deletes only the note_files join rows (line 903); deleteNotebook (lines 622-643) deletes only notebook_files join rows. There is no 'DELETE FROM files' anywhere in Sources/ or Tests/, and the only NoteFileStore.delete calls are attach-failure rollbacks in NoteService+Files.swift (lines 49/103/159/206/253). Every attach mints a unique fileId (no dedup/sharing), so after note/notebook deletion the files rows and on-disk blobs under <noteRoot>/files/ (or S3 objects) are permanently orphaned with no GC or cleanup command. Callers (CLI riela note delete -> GraphQL deleteNote/deleteNotebook -> service) add no compensating blob cleanup.

confirmed.title: Agent answer is discarded when auto-save fails after a successful turn
confirmed.detail: In submitDraft (non-temp path), the turn is only appended to `turns` after saveOrAppend succeeds (lines 54-60). If answerNoteAgentTurn succeeds but persistence fails (locked DB from a concurrent CLI write), the computed answer with citations is thrown away — the user sees only the error banner and must resubmit, even though a valid answer existed. The draft is restored, but the answer is not shown.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Append the turn to the transcript first, then attempt persistence and mark it unsaved on failure (reusing the existing unsaved-turn bookkeeping) so the answer is displayed and can be saved later.
confirmed.verify_note: Confirmed by reading Sources/RielaNoteUI/RielaNoteAgentViewModel.swift lines 53-66: in the non-temporary path, `turns.append(turn)` (line 59) executes only after `saveOrAppend` (line 56) succeeds. If `answerNoteAgentTurn` succeeds but `saveOrAppend` throws (both underlying client calls are throwing), the catch block restores the draft and sets a failed state, but the computed answer is discarded — never shown or retained. No caller guard or retry prevents this, and no test covers persistence failure after a successful answer. The view model already supports unsaved turns (empty persistedNoteIds used by saveTemporaryConversation), so the turn could have been kept and persisted later, making this a genuine low-severity UX defect rather than an unavoidable design constraint.
